title: Pilot deployment failure — request for trial top-up & extension
preview:
From: Priya Sharma <priya@brightlane.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 25 Mar 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Pilot deployment failed during rollout — need runway/top-up
Attachments: error-log.tar.gz (log archive); deployment-screenshot.png (png)

Sean —

Quick note from the pilot team: the latest deployment we ran this morning hit a runtime error during model initialization and failed the canary checks. That blocked the scheduled run of the ingest job and left our QA team unable to validate the connector. We attached the server logs and a screenshot of the service UI that shows the crash loop.

We were planning to finish acceptance tests this week and move to paid staging next week, but with this interruption the pilot schedule is slipping. Given the delay, the execs are asking whether Redwood can extend the pilot window by 30 days and provide a small top-up of free credits (we're estimating ~40k tokens) so our engineers can continue integration work while we sort the deployment issue.

Can you advise on process and whether procurement will need a formal PO amendment? We're pressured to show progress to our CFO on Monday. Appreciate any expedited help.

Thanks,
Priya Sharma
Program Manager, BrightLane
priya@brightlane.com

--
BrightLane Pilot Team


From: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Sharma <priya@brightlane.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 25 Mar 2027 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pilot deployment failed during rollout — need runway/top-up

Priya — thanks for the quick heads-up and the logs. Sorry to hear the canary failed — I know how tight your timeline is.

Looping in Marissa (customer success) and Ethan (SRE) to triage the logs and recommend a short-term runway + credit top-up if appropriate. Marissa — can we escalate approvals here? If we can get a provisional 30-day extension + a 50k token sandbox top-up, that should cover their validation run and give the engineers breathing room. If procurement needs a PO addendum we'll coordinate.

Priya, I'll keep you posted as we triage.

Best,
Sean Gallagher
Account Exec | Redwood Inference
sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com
Mobile: (415) 555-0189

>

Given the delay, the execs are asking whether Redwood can extend the pilot window by 30 days and provide a small top-up of free credits (we're estimating ~40k tokens) so our engineers can continue integration work while we sort the deployment issue.
> 
> Can you advise on process and whether procurement will need a formal PO amendment? We're pressured to show progress to our CFO on Monday. Appreciate any expedited help.
> 
> Thanks,
> Priya


From: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Priya Sharma <priya@brightlane.com>
Date: Thu, 25 Mar 2027 12:20:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Pilot deployment failed — initial technical analysis attached

Sean / Marissa —

I've pulled the logs Priya sent and correlated them with our runtime traces. Summary: an edge-case in the model initialization path causes oversized KV cache allocation when the connector attempts a cold-start with their ingest payload shape. This triggers an OOM and the process restarts; canary assertions then fail.

Immediate mitigations we can apply from our side:
- Pin a smaller KV cache ceiling for BrightLane's pilot deployment (temporary config override).
- Provide a patched runtime build that skips the aggressive prefetch step (low-risk, 1–2 hour deployment).
- If we grant them a mid-term buffer (30 days) plus token top-up, engineering can run the patched build and BrightLane can complete validation without consuming their committed quota.

I've attached: pilot-MAP-v2.pdf (plan for patch + rollback), and a gzipped excerpt of the relevant logs (error-log.tar.gz). If we approve a credit top-up, recommend 50k tokens to be safe given their test volume and retries.

Can Marissa approve provisional credits and extension while we stage the patch? If procurement needs legal sign-off for any contract amendment, flag it now and we can fast-track a short addendum.

Ethan Park
SRE — Inference Platform
ethan_park@redwoodinference.com

On Thu, Mar 25, 2027 at 10:05 AM Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com> wrote:
> Priya — thanks for the quick heads-up and the logs. Sorry to hear the canary failed — I know how tight your timeline is.
> 
> Looping in Marissa (customer success) and Ethan (SRE) to triage the logs and recommend a short-term runway + credit top-up if appropriate. Marissa — can we escalate approvals here? If we can get a provisional 30-day extension + a 50k token sandbox top-up, that should cover their validation run and give the engineers breathing room. If procurement needs a PO addendum we'll coordinate.


From: Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Priya Sharma <priya@brightlane.com>, Sean Gallagher <sean_gallagher@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 25 Mar 2027 16:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Pilot deployment failure — temporary extension and token top-up

Priya / Sean —

Thanks for the clear summary, Ethan — and Priya, I'm sorry for the disruption to your pilot week. I've approved the following provisional remedies to keep your team moving while engineering rolls out the patch:

- 30-day extension to the pilot window starting today (new end date: 2027-04-24).
- One-time 50,000 token sandbox credit applied to your BrightLane pilot account (visible in the console within 30 minutes).
- Engineering will deploy Ethan's patched runtime to your staging cluster; we'll coordinate an overnight slot to minimize impact.

Conditions: this is a one-off remedial top-up tied to the root-cause outlined by Ethan. If further non-root-cause-related usage is requested we will evaluate additional support with Procurement. Please review the attached Pilot MAP plan and shared drive link for the rollout schedule.

Priya — please confirm receipt and whether the overnight window (10pm–12am PT tonight) works for your team. If procurement still requires an addendum I'll work with Legal to put a short amendment on DocuSign (link below).

DocuSign (pilot addendum): https://www.docusign.com/basic/abc123
Drive folder: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1A2B3CpilotMAP
HubSpot deal: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/12345/deal/4819

Best,
Marissa Cole
Senior Customer Success Manager | Redwood Inference
marissa_cole@redwoodinference.com

Attachments referenced: pilot-MAP-v2.pdf; error-log.tar.gz; deployment-screenshot.png

title: Re: Demo scheduling for FinLytics — availability & quick agenda
Security & retention items for MAP and DPA — next steps
preview:
From: Claire Huang <claire.huang@harborpoint.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Mar 2027 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Follow-up: MAP deliverables + security questions

Thanks again for the walkthrough this morning. Per our conversation, I attached a first-pass MAP (MAP_v0.1.xlsx) and the SIG/CAIQ answers you shared — thanks for sending that so quickly. A few things Harborpoint needs to lock in before procurement can move the PO: 

- SOC 2 scope: we need to confirm which Trust Services Criteria are in-scope (Security is required; Availability and Confidentiality likely needed for our use). Do you have a recent SOC 2 report and a summary of controls we can share with our audit team?
- DPA redline: our legal team will deliver a redline but want to confirm Redwood’s proposed data retention windows and right to escrow keys if needed.
- Data residency & hosting: for production we require EU residency for EU customer data. Would Redwood Private (VPC) be an option or will Hosted with EU region suffice?
- Retention windows: procurement is asking for a 7-year retention clause for certain transaction/audit logs. Is that technically feasible? Any implications for cost or architecture?

Target timing from Harborpoint is a production go-live by 2027-06-20; we understand that means a compact eval and evidence sprint. Can we get a short MAP that maps evidence ownership and dates? Also propose a 30–45 minute working session tomorrow to triage evidence items — Wed 03/17 preferred.

Attachments: MAP_v0.1.xlsx, SIG-CAIQ_answers.pdf

Thanks,
Claire Huang
Senior IT Compliance Manager
Harborpoint Financial



From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Claire Huang <claire.huang@harborpoint.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Mar 2027 11:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Follow-up: MAP deliverables + security questions

Claire — really appreciate the clear ask and the timeline. High-level responses + plan below, then I added a compact MAP we can iterate on (attached MAP_v0.2.xlsx).

Summary answers:
- SOC 2: we have a Type II report from 2026 covering Security and Availability; Confidentiality and Processing Integrity are in progress but not currently in scope for the 2026 report. We can provide the SOC 2 report under NDA and a control narrative + pen test summary.
- DPA & retention: Redwood supports flexible retention tiers. Practically we differentiate: ephemeral request data (30 days by default), aggregated logs (3 years), and immutable audit logs (configurable, can support 7 years but may require archival + additional cost). We will note the 7-year requirement in the MAP and call out cost delta.
- Residency & hosting: Hosted EU region covers data residency for storage and processing. For customers requiring private network isolation we recommend Redwood Private (VPC) — we can include a deployment appendix for that in the MAP.

Proposed compact MAP (attached MAP_v0.2.xlsx):
Phase 1 (Eval / sandbox) — 2 weeks: test app, validate EU region and auth flows.
Phase 2 (Evidence sprint) — 4–6 weeks: SOC2 evidence intake, SIG/CAIQ follow-up, DPA redline cycle with Legal.
Phase 3 (Sign & onboard) — 2–3 weeks: finalize contract, provision Private if requested, run smoke tests.

Action items: 
- Ethan: share SOC2 report and pen test summary (will load to drive and share link).
- Neha: draft evidence intake checklist and schedule working session.
- Harborpoint: send DPA redline and confirm required retention scope per data class.

I’ve also linked the HubSpot deal for reference: https://app.hubspot.com/deals/12345. Proposed working session: Wed Mar 17, 10:00–10:30 PT. Does that work?

Attachments: MAP_v0.2.xlsx

Best,
Ethan Park
Head of Growth, Redwood Inference


From: Claire Huang <claire.huang@harborpoint.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lee <marcus.lee@harborpoint.com>, Legal Ops <legal@harborpoint.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Mar 2027 12:35:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Follow-up: MAP deliverables + security questions

Thanks Ethan — that helps. A couple clarifications from our side: 

- We will send a DPA redline today (DPA_redline_v1.docx). Primary asks: 7-year retention on audit logs, encryption-at-rest with customer-managed keys for production, and an evergreen incident notification SLA (48 hours).
- For SOC 2, our auditors will need the control matrix and recent pen test artifacts. Please note they typically want evidence mapped by control ID.
- On residency: Harborpoint will accept Hosted (EU region) for the pilot, but production customers in certain lines (payments) will require Private/VPC. Please include an appendix for Private provisioning lead times and estimated monthly cost.

We can confirm Wed 03/17 10:00 PT for the working session — will include Legal and a technical lead from Harborpoint.

Attachments: DPA_redline_v1.docx

Claire



From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>
To: Claire Huang <claire.huang@harborpoint.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Mar 2027 15:10:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Follow-up: MAP deliverables + security questions

Claire — looping in a compact evidence intake to make the working session productive. I attached a one-page SOC2-evidence-checklist.pdf; below are the items I’d like Harborpoint to confirm ownership of before Wed: 

Requested pre-read / confirmations: 
- Which data classes require 7-year retention (transactional audit logs vs. full request payloads)? Please enumerate.
- Are customer-managed keys mandatory for production or preferred? If mandatory, we’ll include KMS integration notes and lead time in the appendix.
- Legal redline:please highlight 2–3 must-change clauses so we can prioritize during the working session (we’ll pre-review).

Suggested retention tiering we propose to include in the MAP: 
- Ephemeral request payloads: default 30 days (can be shortened).
- Aggregated usage/metrics: 3 years (default).
- Immutable audit records (transaction logs, admin audits): configurable up to 7 years — implement via cold-archive (S3 Glacier / equivalent) and key escrow options where required.

Prep material I’ll upload to the shared drive before 6pm PT: SOC2 control matrix (redwood_control_matrix_2026.pdf), pen test summary, and the MAP_v0.2.xlsx we discussed. Please confirm attendees for Wed 10:00 PT (we’ll send calendar invite).

Attachments: SOC2-evidence-checklist.pdf

Thanks,
Neha Kapoor
Director, Security & Trust
Redwood Inference



From: Claire Huang <claire.huang@harborpoint.com>
To: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwoodinference.com>, Marcus Lee <marcus.lee@harborpoint.com>, Legal Ops <legal@harborpoint.com>
Date: Tue, 16 Mar 2027 15:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Follow-up: MAP deliverables + security questions

Appreciate the clear tiers, Neha — that structure will help procurement. I’ll confirm the retention classes (payments audit logs = 7 years; user activity traces = 3 years; request payloads = 30 days) and highlight three redline priorities for Legal. We’ll join Wed 10:00 PT and expect the drive pre-reads.

I’ll also add Marcus (technical lead) to the invite. See you tomorrow.

Claire


<end of thread>

title: Hosted API performance: P95/P99 skew linked to request queue buildup (PivotalCare)
preview:
From: Alice Reynolds <alice.reynolds@pivotalcare.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 11 Mar 2027 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: Increased P95/P99 latency over last 3 hours — production

Hi Ben,

We're seeing a sudden increase in tail latency from your Hosted API for our production tenant. P95 has climbed from ~220ms to ~1.2s and P99 is now consistently 2.5-3s for text.generate calls. This started about 3 hours ago and coincides with a higher error spike (5xx ~0.6%). Throughput is otherwise steady.

We rely on the 99th percentile for user-facing features and need this mitigated quickly. I've attached a short sample of traces and the aggregated P95/P99 export. Can your team confirm whether this is related to batching or request queueing?

Thanks,
Alice Reynolds
Head of Platform Operations
PivotalCare

[Attachment: pivotalcare_p95_p99_run_20270311.csv (text/csv)]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Alice Reynolds <alice.reynolds@pivotalcare.com>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 11 Mar 2027 09:03:22 -0700
Subject: Re: Increased P95/P99 latency over last 3 hours — production

Hi Alice — thanks for the heads up and attachment. We’re treating this as a Sev2 and starting a focused triage.

Immediate notes:
- We’ve seen similar symptoms before when queue depth and batching align poorly with sudden request fan-in: p95 rises while overall throughput is unchanged.
- I’m pulling the request queue snapshots and recent kernel/batching metrics now.
- Naomi is pulling logs for 5xx patterns to see if the errors map to specific model variants or nodes.

Can you confirm whether the affected endpoints include streamed responses or function-calling flows? That changes how we treat KV cache pressure.

Ben
Ben Carter
Sr. Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference

[Attachment: p95_trace_0311.txt (text/plain)]

From: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 11 Mar 2027 09:21:10 -0700
Subject: Re: Increased P95/P99 latency over last 3 hours — production

Ben/Marissa — preliminary log correlation: several inference nodes show a sustained queue depth increase starting 11:02Z. Batching sizes concurrently rose by ~2.1x on some routes; autoscaler did not add capacity due to a short-lived spike threshold. No memory OOMs, but KV cache hit ratio dropped ~8% which would increase decode path latency.

I’m attaching the request-queue snapshot and a couple of raw traces that show requests waiting in queue then being batched into large groups. Suggest follow-ups: shorten max batch window for PivotalCare routes and temporarily route them to lower-batch, higher-replica pools.

Naomi

[Attachment: request-queue-snapshot-0311.json]

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 11 Mar 2027 10:05:44 -0700
Subject: Re: Increased P95/P99 latency over last 3 hours — production

Team — quick plan for mitigation while we continue root-cause:
1) Apply immediate rule: reduce max_batch_window_ms from 40 -> 10 for the PivotalCare routing policy.
2) Spin up two extra replicas in the US-hosted pool for the next 60 minutes (manual burst).
3) Enable prefix caching for frequent short prompts (PivotalCare has a high prefix hit candidate set).

Ethan/ops: can you run the rollout? Ben, please communicate the temporary impact and ETA to Alice. I’ll monitor for any increase in 5xxs from the deployment.

Marissa
Director, Platform Ops

On Thu, Mar 11, 2027 at 09:03, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Alice — thanks for the heads up and attachment. We’re treating this as a Sev2 and starting a focused triage.


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Alice Reynolds <alice.reynolds@pivotalcare.com>
Cc: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 11 Mar 2027 10:22:09 -0700
Subject: Re: Increased P95/P99 latency over last 3 hours — production

Hi Alice — quick update: we identified a short window where request arrivals triggered larger-than-expected batch coalescing which increased queue hold times for low-latency requests. We’re applying a temporary routing policy: lower batch window + extra replicas. Expected to see P95 drop within ~8-12 minutes after the change.

What to expect: a small increase in cost due to extra replicas and reduced batching efficiency; latency should improve for tail percentiles. We’ll keep you posted and provide the post-mortem once root cause is finalized.

Ben

[Attachment: scheduled-change-notes.txt (text/plain)]

From: Alice Reynolds <alice.reynolds@pivotalcare.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: ops@pivotalcare.com
Date: Fri, 12 Mar 2027 02:47:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Increased P95/P99 latency over last 3 hours — production

Ben/Team — thanks for the fast turnaround. I can confirm P95 is back down to ~260-280ms and P99 under 1.1s after your changes. We'll expect the post-mortem and any config recommendations for long-term stability (batch tuning, routing policies, recommended autoscaler thresholds).

If you can include a short list of guardrails we can add on our side (client-side concurrency limits / request pacing) that would be helpful.

Appreciate the responsiveness — Alice

On Thu, Mar 11, 2027 at 10:22, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Alice — quick update: we identified a short window where request arrivals triggered larger-than-expected batch coalescing which increased queue hold times for low-latency requests. We’re applying a temporary routing policy: lower batch window + extra replicas. Expected to see P95 drop within ~8-12 minutes after the change.

title: Coord: Acme procurement intake — slot windows & security checklist
preview:
From: Rachel Kim <rachel.kim@redwood.ai>
To: Mark Jenkins <mark.jenkins@acme.com>, procurement@acme.com
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 10 Mar 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Coord: Acme procurement intake — slot windows & security checklist

Hi Mark / Acme procurement team,

Thanks for the interest — excited to get the procurement intake and initial security checklist aligned so procurement, legal, and your security team have what they need. To keep this efficient I want to propose two short sessions we can run next week: 

1) Commercial & procurement intake (45–60m): PO terms, billing cadence, SOW overview, DocuSign process, procurement questions
2) Technical & security sync (60m): network allowlist, data flow, KMS/PII handling, SOC2 evidence requests (SE + security)

Proposed time windows (all PT):
- Tue 16 Mar: 10:00–12:00 or 15:00–17:00
- Wed 17 Mar: 09:00–11:00 or 14:00–16:00

Please reply with 1–2 preferred windows and who from your side will attend each session (procurement contact, security lead, and any engineers). I attached a one-page procurement_timeline.xlsx and a draft security_checklist.pdf for reference.

Suggested agenda (procurement session):
- Introductions (5m)
- Commercial terms & billing (15m)
- PO/DocuSign timeline (10m)
- Next steps & action owners (15m)

If you'd rather collapse into a single 75–90m meeting we can do that too — let me know.

Thanks,
Rachel
Rachel Kim
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference
rachel.kim@redwood.ai

Attachments: procurement_timeline.xlsx (spreadsheet stub), security_checklist.pdf (doc stub)

Mark

title: Kickoff: Medilabs pilot MAP and evaluation roadmap
preview:
From: Nikhil Sharma <nikhil.sharma@redwood.com>
To: Ayesha Rahman <ayesha.rahman@medilabs.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Grace Oconnor <grace.oconnor@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 10 Mar 2027 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Kickoff: Medilabs pilot MAP and evaluation roadmap

Hi Ayesha —

title: Dedicated pool renewal: proposed capacity increase and operational safeguards
preview:
From: Natalie Chen <natalie@aurora.ai>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 2 Mar 2027 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Renewal discussion — Dedicated pool + capacity increase

Hi Logan,

We're getting close to our Dedicated pool renewal window (contract expires 2027-04-30) and procurement asked me to start the conversation early. Our usage has been trending up — we currently have an 8-GPU reserved pool under commit DL-8491 but are looking to upsell to 16 GPUs for steady throughput starting in Q2.

Key asks from our side:
- Updated monthly commit and list pricing for 16 GPUs (what does billing look like per-month vs hourly?)
- Throughput guarantees (p50/p95 latency targets for typical gen workloads) and any rebate language for SLA misses
- Activation/phase-in options (we may want a staggered turn-up to avoid immediate budget jump)
- Procurement paperwork: PO and DocuSign preferred.

Can you propose next steps and a draft amendment? I can loop in procurement after we have numbers.

Thanks,
Natalie Chen
VP, AI Platforms
Aurora Labs



From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Natalie Chen <natalie@aurora.ai>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 3 Mar 2027 10:28:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal discussion — Dedicated pool + capacity increase

Hi Natalie — thanks for the heads-up and congrats on the usage growth. Happy to put together a clear amendment and rollout plan. High-level proposal below; attaching a draft amendment and a pricing breakdown for your review.

Proposal (summary):
- Move from 8 → 16 GPUs on Dedicated pool DL-8491.
- Monthly committed units: equivalent of 16 x reserved GPUs (billed as committed monthly units). For simplicity we can express commitment as 16 GPUs * 720 committed hours = 11,520 GPU-hours/month.
- Monthly recurring fee: $128,000 (this is the committed price; equivalent to $8,000 per GPU-month).
- Optional phased activation: Activate 8 GPUs immediately, additional 8 GPUs scheduled over a 4–6 week window; billing pro-rated by activation date.
- Throughput & latency guardrails: baseline p50 latency 70ms; p95 latency 260ms for 2k token gen on our baseline m-family models. We propose a 60-day monitoring window post-activation to validate traffic profile and then lock SLOs.
- Burst capacity & fallbacks: 25% burst headroom available for short periods; if sustained above threshold we will recommend surge add-on or short-term overage billing.

Next steps:
1) You review the attached amendment (Aurora_Amendment_v2.pdf) and pricing sheet.
2) Confirm preferred activation schedule and whether procurement wants a single PO or phased POs for the two activations.
3) I can produce a DocuSign packet once we align on language; Marissa (CS) will own the activation runbook and Ethan (Finance) will handle billing terms.

Attachments: Aurora_Amendment_v2.pdf, pricing_breakdown_aurora.xlsx

Thanks — I can hop on a quick 30m sync this week if useful.

— Logan Wright
Account Exec, Redwood Inference


From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>, Natalie Chen <natalie@aurora.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 4 Mar 2027 08:07:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal discussion — Dedicated pool + capacity increase

Quick technical clarifications from my side to make sure the SLO language is actionable:

Natalie — a few questions that affect throughput/price recommendations:
- Typical model mix (Llama2-style vs larger 70B models)?
- Average sequence length and RPS distribution (are you bursty on mornings or steady?)
- Whether you rely heavily on KV cache/prefix reuse — that changes effective throughput significantly.

Operational suggestions:
- If you plan to run larger models >70B, we should reserve an additional GPU headroom for memory-bound inference or use multi-instance sharding (pricing differs).
- Recommend a 60-day measurement window after each activation step to set p95 buckets; apply rebate only if we miss by >2 full days in a 30-day period.
- Offer: a one-time credit equal to 5% of the first full month committed fee if you sign by 2027-03-24. That helps procurement close the current budget quarter.

I can draft tightened SLA/rebate language for the amendment once we know model mix.

— Karthik
Solutions Architect, Redwood Inference


From: Natalie Chen <natalie@aurora.ai>
To: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
Cc: procurement@aurora.ai
Date: Sat, 6 Mar 2027 11:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Renewal discussion — Dedicated pool + capacity increase

Thanks Logan — this is exactly the level of detail procurement needs. A few items from procurement side: 

Procurement asks:
- Can you provide a final rate card + the DocuSign-ready amendment by next Wednesday (2027-03-10)?
- Procurement prefers a single PO that covers the full 16-GPU commit, with Redwood providing a 30-day pro-rated invoice for the second activation if we phase.
- Include SOC2 evidence and data residency details (we'll need to run through a short questionnaire). Attaching the procurement questionnaire; please have your security team confirm the items.

SLA note: procurement asked if you can include an explicit monthly rebate cap (example: rebates capped at 10% of monthly commitment) and a 30-day cure period. Also please confirm support hours and escalation path.

Opening for negotiation: Aurora is open to a 12-month renewal term if there is a 7.5% discount on the committed price and an option to re-evaluate in 6 months for additional burst blocks.

Attaching: SOC2_evidence_request.pdf

— Natalie
VP, AI Platforms, Aurora Labs


From: Logan Wright <logan.wright@redwood.com>
To: Natalie Chen <natalie@aurora.ai>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 10 Mar 2027 16:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Renewal discussion — Dedicated pool + capacity increase

Natalie — appreciate the quick turn. Per procurement asks we attached an updated amendment and a DocuSign packet: https://docusign.com/link/EXAMPLE_DOCUSIGN_AURORA_8491. Key points in the attached amendment (Aurora_Amendment_v2.pdf):

- Commit: 16-GPU reserved pool for a 12-month term starting 2027-04-01.
- Billing: Monthly committed fee $118,400 after a 7.5% renewal discount (applies to the committed fee only). Pro-rated invoicing for phase-in activation as requested.
- SLA/Rebates: p50/p95 targets as discussed. Rebate clause included with 30-day cure period; total rebates capped at 10% of monthly commitment.
- Activation plan: Phase A (8 GPUs) active 2027-04-01; Phase B (additional 8 GPUs) scheduled by 2027-04-22 with pro-rated billing.
- Onboarding: Marissa will run the activation runbook; Karthik will validate throughput against your model mix during the 60-day measurement windows.
- SOC2 & security: We confirmed the requested artifacts; security will reach out to procurement separately.

If this reflects your expectations, reply here with procurement's PO instructions and we'll send the DocuSign packet for signature. Once signed, we will provision Phase A immediately and queue Phase B per agreed date.

Thanks — happy to join a short call if there are final redlines.

— Logan Wright
Account Exec, Redwood Inference

title: Carrier rationalization & shipping cutover: timeline and SLAs
preview:
From: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.ai>
To: Claire Roberts <claire@workspacepartners.com>, sales@nimbustel.com, ops@shipfast.com
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 2 Mar 2027 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: Carrier rationalization & shipping cutover: timeline and SLAs

Hi all,

We're starting the carrier rationalization effort for Redwood's US workplace program: consolidate telecom accounts, standardize laptop shipping logistics for new hires, and lock a cutover window for our January-to-Q2 onboarding surge. High level ask: we need a coordinated plan that covers (1) SIM/carrier porting & number consolidation, (2) bulk laptop shipment SLA for both staged and expedited deliveries, and (3) a dry-run/test cutover in one region.

Attached is a working matrix and a proposed cutover timeline (v2). Please review and call out any hard blockers or assumptions by EOD Thursday. Key constraints from procurement: 30-day PO lead time and invoice terms 30/Net.

Attachments: redwood_carrier_rationalization_matrix.xlsx; shipping_cutover_timeline_v2.xlsx

Next steps I expect:
- Nimbus: feasibility and carrier-level lead times + any porting fees
- ShipFast: SLA for pallet vs individual shipments, expedited rates for onboarding kits
- Claire/Workspace Partners: hotdesk telecom handoff and on-site contact for dry-run

Thanks,
Logan
Logan Wright
Head of Workplace Operations
Redwood Inference


From: Claire Roberts <claire@workspacepartners.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 2 Mar 2027 11:03:00 -04:00
Subject: Re: Carrier rationalization & shipping cutover: timeline and SLAs

Thanks Logan — got it. Two quick clarifying items before we scope the dry-run:

1) For the dry-run, do you want us to stand up a temporary hotdesk site or run it entirely from our existing NYC site?
2) Are personal numbers being ported (employees keep numbers) or are we moving to company-managed eSIMs?

We can support on-site coordination and provide a single point of contact (onsite lead) for the cutover day. I'll put together a list of on-site logistics tasks and share by Wednesday morning.

Best,
Claire Roberts
Program Lead, Workplace Partners


From: Nimbus Telecom Sales <sales@nimbustel.com>
To: Logan Wright <logan@redwood.ai>, Claire Roberts <claire@workspacepartners.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 2 Mar 2027 13:26:00 -05:00
Subject: Re: Carrier rationalization & shipping cutover: timeline and SLAs

Logan — appreciate the heads up. Quick responses:

- Porting: typical port-out window is 7–10 business days per block of numbers; bulk port programs can compress this to 4–6 days if we get signed LOAs and authorization templates in advance.
- eSIM vs physical SIM: we support both. eSIM provisioning has a faster activation path for new hires (same-day activation in many cases) but requires device compatibility.
- Costs: porting fees vary; estimate $0.50–$2.00/number depending on carrier. I'll attach a short rate card in my follow-up.

Obstacle to note: if Redwood wants to consolidate billing across three legacy accounts into a single master account, we'll need billing reconciliation data for the last 6 months. Can your procurement team share?

Regards,
Daniel Cho

title: Runtime degradation: customer-facing update and action plan
preview:
From: Lina Torres <lina@fastcommerce.com>
To: Mina Haddad <mina_haddad@redwood.com>
Cc: ops@fastcommerce.com
Date: Tue, 2 Mar 2027 09:17:03 -0700
Subject: Production latency spike impacting checkout (priority)

Hi Mina,

We started seeing elevated latency from your API around 09:05 PST — checkout calls are spiking to 2.5–3s and a subset are timing out. This is impacting live traffic in us-west-2 (approx 12% of requests failing). Can you confirm if Redwood is experiencing any degradation?

We rely on the /v1/generate endpoint with model rwd-7b. Attaching a sample of failing request IDs below.

Request IDs: fc-7d9a, fc-7d9b, fc-7dda

Thanks,
Lina
Lina Torres
Head of Platform, FastCommerce
[Attachment: sample-reqs.txt (text)]


From: Mina Haddad <mina_haddad@redwood.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 2 Mar 2027 09:34:41 -0700
Subject: Fwd: Production latency spike impacting checkout (priority)

Team — forwarding an urgent customer report from FastCommerce (Lina Torres). They’re seeing elevated latency and increased timeout rate in us-west-2 impacting checkout flows.

Immediate asks:
- Ethan: can you confirm infrastructure / region health and whether any autoscaler events, node preemptions, or kernel updates occurred around 09:00–09:20?
- Marcus: please check model-serving telemetry for rwd-7b (kv-cache pressure, batching, queue depth).
- Priyom: prepare a short customer-ready status line and indicate ETA for a mitigation.

I’ve CC’d Laura for visibility given customer impact. I’ll keep Lina updated every 30 minutes until we have root cause or a mitigation.

Thanks,
Mina
Account Lead, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: redwood-ops-notes-0302.txt (text)]

---------- Forwarded message ---------
From: Lina Torres <lina@fastcommerce.com>
Date: Tue, 2 Mar 2027 09:17:03 -0700
Subject: Production latency spike impacting checkout (priority)
To: Mina Haddad <mina_haddad@redwood.com>
Cc: ops@fastcommerce.com

Hi Mina,...


From: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.com>
To: Mina Haddad <mina_haddad@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 2 Mar 2027 09:47:12 -0700
Subject: Re: Fwd: Production latency spike impacting checkout (priority)

On Tue, Mar 2, 2027 at 09:34 Mina Haddad <mina_haddad@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team — forwarding an urgent customer report from FastCommerce...

Quick update from infra: we had an autoscaler churn event in us-west-2 starting at 09:02 where two preemptible nodes were reclaimed and replaced. The replacement nodes came up but we observed a short period where routing split traffic across fewer warm instances, which caused queue growth and higher tail latency for some streams. No control-plane changes.

Actions taken:
- Manually increased headroom for the FastCommerce org pool (scale +2 active replicas).
- Forced KV-cache warmups for rwd-7b shards serving that org.
- Temporarily widened batching window to reduce timeouts for long-running calls.

Impact: we saw latency tail reduce after 09:22 and error rate fall, but FastCommerce still reported intermittent failures through 09:35. Investigating why warmup didn’t fully mitigate for a subset of long-context requests.

Ethan
SRE, Redwood Inference


From: Mina Haddad <mina_haddad@redwood.com>
To: Lina Torres <lina@fastcommerce.com>, Kyle Reynolds <kyle.reynolds@fastcommerce.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom_das@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 2 Mar 2027 10:05:00 -0700
Subject: Redwood status update — mitigation in progress (us-west-2)

Lina / Kyle —

Thank you for the quick report and sample request IDs. Our SRE team identified a transient node preemption/autoscaler churn in us-west-2 that caused a temporary reduction in warm serving capacity for rwd-7b. Immediate mitigations have been applied: +2 replicas to your org pool, KV-cache warmups, and a temporary batching window increase. Early telemetry shows tail latency and error rate coming down; we expect the bulk of impact to be resolved within 30–45 minutes.

What we’re doing next:
- Root cause analysis: we’re collecting full node/instance events and model-serving logs; ETA for preliminary RCA is 48 hours.
- Short-term risk mitigation: we will add a buffer to the FastCommerce pool to reduce sensitivity to node churn for 24 hours.
- Customer safety: if you prefer, we can failover your traffic to a compatible model variant (rwd-7b-compact) while we stabilize. That would reduce latency at a small quality cost. Let us know if you want us to proceed.

We’ll send a 30-minute status note and then a formal post-incident summary within 48 hours. Apologies for the impact; we’ll treat this with high priority.

Attachments: latency-heatmap-0302.png (observed tail before/after mitigation), redwood-runtime-logs-0302.zip (ops logs).

Best,
Mina Haddad
Account Lead, Redwood Inference
+1 415-555-0123


From: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.com>
To: Lina Torres <lina@fastcommerce.com>, Mina Haddad <mina_haddad@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 4 Mar 2027 16:05:00 -0700
Subject: Executive summary and next steps — FastCommerce incident (Mar 2)

Lina / FastCommerce leadership —

I’m Laura Bennett, VP of Accounts at Redwood. I’m following up with a short executive summary of the incident on Mar 2 and the actions we’ve taken. Thank you for your collaboration through the incident response.

Summary:
- Incident window: 09:02–09:35 PST on 2027-03-02.
- Symptom: increased tail latency and elevated error rate impacting ~12% of checkout calls in us-west-2.
- Immediate cause: autoscaler churn and two preemptible nodes reclaimed; resulting shortfall in warm serving capacity for rwd-7b.

Customer impact & mitigation:
- We increased dedicated pool headroom and warmed KV caches; error rate returned near baseline by ~09:22 and residual intermittent failures cleared by ~09:35.
- No data exposure or request content loss was observed.
- Offer: we provided a compatibility failover to rwd-7b-compact and recommended a 24-hour pool-buffer while we implement a longer-term fix. FastCommerce requested the buffer and accepted the optional failover.

Planned follow-ups:
1) Short-term (24–72 hrs): add pool headroom automation for org pools using preemption risk signals.
2) Medium-term (2–4 weeks): change allocation policy to avoid allocation of preemptible-only node placements for performance-critical org pools; improve warmup orchestration for large-model shards.
3) Postmortem: we will share a technical RCA and remediation timeline in a formal postmortem doc by 2027-03-04 (attached draft).

Compensation: as a gesture of goodwill for the disruption we are offering a credit of one week of dedicated capacity for FastCommerce. Finance and your account team will follow up to apply the credit.

Attachments: postmortem-draft-fastcommerce-0304.pdf (technical RCA draft), redwood-runtime-logs-0302.zip

If you’d like a 30-minute readout with our engineering leads, we can schedule one tomorrow morning.

Sincerely,
Laura Bennett
VP, Accounts — Redwood Inference
laura_bennett@redwood.com
[Attachment: DocuSign-link for credit acknowledgment (sent 2027-03-04 09:00 PST)]

Hi Natalie —

Thanks again for the productive call earlier today. Quick recap from my side and the items we agreed to move forward with so we can prep an accurate SOW/MA: 

- Scope: MedInsights will need Redwood to support HIPAA-covered processing for PII/PHI plus SOC 2 compliance for the tenant; exploration of ISO 27001 as a longer-term requirement.
- SOC 2: you indicated an existing Type II report from 2026 but want Redwood to confirm the controls mapping and any additional controls for hosting PHI.
- Questionnaire: you asked for a completed vendor security questionnaire; I can share our cover sheet + current SOC 2 statement redacted.
- Timeline & next steps: you requested a 2-week window to review docs internally and an initial answers pack by next Friday (Feb 26).

I'm attaching a short cover sheet that explains our standard security artifacts and a template for the vendor questionnaire. Once we get your completed questionnaire back we'll triage anything that looks like a policy gap and map required compensating controls/contract language.

Tessa Morgan

From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>

Subject: Draft: formal performance pathway for Ethan Park

Laura / Marissa —

I put together a first-pass PIP-style plan for Ethan (attached) so we can align manager/HR/legal before we meet with him. High-level context: Ethan has repeatedly missed OKR deliverables in H2 and H1, escalation conversations occurred in Nov/Dec, and the last 1:1 in January resulted in a written warning. Goal here is to create a timeboxed plan with clear success criteria and supportive checkpoints.

Things I want from HR/legal before I share with Ethan:
- Laura: confirm tone and any mandatory language we must include from policy.
- Marissa: confirm timeline aligns with comp/benefits/career consequences guidance.
- Legal (if needed): quick read for any phrasing that could create risk around termination.

Attached: PIP_Formal_Template_v3.docx (my edits), ethan_performance_notes.pdf (timeline + evidence).

I was hoping we could huddle at 11:30 today for 15 mins to decide next steps. If legal input is needed I can loop counsel — otherwise I plan to deliver to Ethan on Wed 2/9 and propose a 60-day performance pathway.

Thanks,
Jordan

Jordan Blake
Director, Product Engineering
Redwood Inference


From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 7 Feb 2027 10:04:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Draft: formal performance pathway for Ethan Park

Thanks for drafting this, Jordan. Quick read attached — a few HR-driven edits and a softened opening paragraph to ensure we keep the conversation coaching-focused while still being explicit about consequences.

Main edits I made in the attached: HR added the timeline of prior interventions, clarified the appeals/representation language, and removed one sentence that read like subjective characterization. Please review.

I think before we meet with Ethan we should have legal glance at the success criteria section where you tie outcomes to role expectations; I'll loop Marcus for a short read. If Marcus thinks language is safe we can proceed on Wed as planned.

I can join the 11:30 huddle.

Laura

--
Laura Bennett
Senior People Partner, Talent & Org
Redwood Inference


From: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.com>

Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 7 Feb 2027 12:30:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Draft: formal performance pathway for Ethan Park

Jordan/Laura —

Quick notes after a 15-min read (attached redline):
- Avoid definitive language that presumes termination (e.g., \"will be terminated\") — keep it conditional based on failure to meet objective criteria.
- Strengthen the documentation and evidence section so that any later personnel action is defensible.
- Add a clause about reasonable accommodations / ADA considerations and point to where employee can request reasonable adjustments.

If HR keeps the revised opening tone this looks fine. I also recommend we keep Marcus (in-house) as the reviewing counsel; I don't think outside counsel is necessary unless we anticipate union/other external risk.

I added a few inline suggestions in legal_comments_redline.docx. Happy to join a quick call.

-Marcus

Marcus Lin
General Counsel (interim)
Redwood Inference

title: Optimize Toolkit — savings draft, quantization scope & ops checklist
preview:
From: Claire Jenkins <claire@cerebra.ai>
To: Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.ai>
Cc: Andres Lopez <andres.lopez@cerebra.ai>, Procurement Team <procurement@cerebra.ai>
Date: Tue, 14 Dec 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Optimize Toolkit — initial savings ask

Thanks for the call earlier, Ethan. Per our internal finance check, we can commit to a 12-month pilot (production traffic limited to search/chat endpoints) but we need clearer guardrails on the savings you quoted — specifically: effective discount bands tied to throughput, and the expected accuracy delta if we apply aggressive quantization (INT8 vs FP16).

Can you share a short deck with: commit tiers, typical TCO delta at 1M tokens/day, recommended quantization per route (search vs chat), and any operational constraints (KV cache warmup, batch sizing guidance)? Also procurement wants to see an ops checklist for on-call/rollback during rollout.

Claire
[Attachment: none]

From: Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.ai>
To: Claire Jenkins <claire@cerebra.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 14 Dec 2026 11:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Optimize Toolkit — savings draft, quantization scope & ops checklist

Claire — thanks, turning this into a concise package. Attached: Optimize_Savings_Draft_v3.pdf (pricing bands + examples), Quant-Options-Matrix.xlsx (latency/quality tradeoffs), and an Ops-Checklist.md for rollout. High-level summary below: 

- Commit tiers (monthly): 0-50k USD (starter), 50-200k USD (growth), >200k USD (enterprise). Discounts scale from 10% / 18% / 28% off list for the Optimize toolkit add-on (applies to tokens billed to optimized models).
- Pilot offer: 3 months credit equal to 15% of committed monthly spend if you sign within 2 weeks.
- Quantization guidance: 
  * Search (short queries, high qps): INT8 recommended with calibrated per-model bias correction. Expected perf: ~2.2x lower cost, latency +5–12ms median. Quality measured by MAP@10: ~0.8–1.5% relative drop on average retrieval workloads.
  * Chat (longer context, structured responses): FP16 or mixed-precision INT8 fallback. We recommend FP16 for gen models unless you can accept a 2–3% hallucination/regression risk.
- Batching/caching suggestions: active prefix caching for repeated prompts (prefix hit rate expected 12–25% in your test corpus), dynamic batch sizing capped by 150ms tail latency SLO.
- Ops considerations: KV cache priming, canary rollout to 5% traffic for 48 hrs, automatic rollback on generation-quality regression (>3% on eval set) or latency SLO breach.

I'll be available this afternoon to walk through the deck. If procurement needs a redlineable SOW, Monica can draft.

Ethan
[Attachment: Optimize_Savings_Draft_v3.pdf; Quant-Options-Matrix.xlsx; Ops-Checklist.md]

From: Andres Lopez <andres.lopez@cerebra.ai>
To: Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.ai>
Cc: Claire Jenkins <claire@cerebra.ai>, Procurement Team <procurement@cerebra.ai>
Date: Tue, 14 Dec 2026 13:27:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Optimize Toolkit — savings draft, quantization scope & ops checklist

Ethan — deck looks good. A couple of clarifying asks from our engineering lead: 

1) For the INT8 path you mention "calibrated per-model bias correction" — can you detail the expected runbook? Who owns calibration runs and how long do they take on a 2k-sequence validation set?
2) For KV cache warmup, is there an API we can call to prefill keys during deployments? We need to avoid cold-start regressions on product launch.

Also, procurement flags the 28% tier as generous but requests a minimum commitment term of 12 months for that band. We are evaluating whether to accept a 9-month pilot then convert or commit to a full year.

-Andres
[Attachment: none]

From: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
To: Andres Lopez <andres.lopez@cerebra.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.ai>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 14 Dec 2026 15:01:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Optimize Toolkit — savings draft, quantization scope & ops checklist

Andres — quick answers: 

1) Calibration runbook: Redwood provides the tooling (open-source kit + managed runner). Process: (a) you supply a representative validation set (we recommend ~2k seqs), (b) calibration job runs on dedicated infra we manage and typically finishes in 2–6 hours depending on model size (Llama2-70B ~5 hrs), (c) we return a bias-correction table and a lightweight wrapper you can deploy. We can run the initial job for you as part of the pilot onboarding (no extra charge). Ownership: Redwood runs the first calibration; ongoing re-calibration is jointly owned — we can include a monthly re-calibration option in the SOW.

2) KV cache prefill: yes — there is a prefill API (POST /v1/kvcache/prefill) in the private console. For hosted customers we can orchestrate prefill during deploy windows; for Private deployments we provide the operation scripts. We'll add the API snippet to the Ops-Checklist.

On the 28% discount: we typically require a 12-month committed band to unlock that level; we can offer a staged path for a 9-month pilot that converts into year 1 at the same terms if conversion occurs within 60 days of pilot end. Will that work?

Monica
[Attachment: Ops-Checklist.md]

From: Claire Jenkins <claire@cerebra.ai>
To: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.ai>
Cc: Andres Lopez <andres.lopez@cerebra.ai>, Procurement Team <procurement@cerebra.ai>
Date: Wed, 15 Dec 2026 16:07:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Optimize Toolkit — savings draft, quantization scope & ops checklist

Monica / Ethan — this is very helpful, thanks. Procurement will take the staged conversion option back to the CFO and legal (they like the conversion clause). Two small follow-ups before we sign: 

- Can you surface one or two example eval prompts and the pre/post INT8 results (we'd like a short appendix in the deck showing MAP/ROUGE deltas)?
- For the onboarding run you offered, will that be included in the 3 months pilot credit or is it additional professional services?

If those are wrapped up, I think we can get a signature on a pilot SOW by the end of next week.

Claire
[Attachment: Appendix-request.txt]

title: Limits on model training and subprocessor approvals — execution path
preview:
From: Samantha Cole <samantha.cole@acadiahealth.com>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.ai>
Cc: Joel Ramirez <joel.ramirez@acadiahealth.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Acadia DPA questions — model training carve-out and subprocessors

Hi Jasmine,

Thanks for taking the time last week. Our legal team reviewed the DPA and flagged two items we need firm commitments on before procurement can sign:

1) No training/use for model improvement: we need an explicit clause that Customer Data (defined as identifiable patient or business records) will not be used to train, improve, or fine-tune base models or product models without Customer's prior written consent. Acceptable exceptions might include aggregated telemetry for service reliability, but those must be pseudonymized and not reversibly identifiable.

2) Subprocessor approvals & data transfer: please provide the full subprocessor matrix and confirm which subprocessors will have access to plaintext Customer Data. For EU-origin data we must see the SCCs or equivalent transfer mechanism; any new subprocessor that will access Customer Data should require 30 days' notice and approval.

We attached a short redline highlighting the proposed no-training language and a CSV with a list of subprocessors we were given during the pilot. Can you confirm whether Redwood can accept: (a) a narrow carve-out for aggregated telemetry only, and (b) execution via DocuSign so procurement can close the PO by month-end?

Cheers,
Samantha Cole
Sr. Counsel, Privacy & Security
Acadia Health

Attachment: Acadia_DPA_redline_20261124.pdf (PDF redline)
Attachment: subprocessor-matrix-20261130.csv (CSV list)

From: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.ai>
To: Samantha Cole <samantha.cole@acadiahealth.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 23 Nov 2026 11:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Acadia DPA questions — model training carve-out and subprocessors

Samantha —

Appreciate the clear asks and the redline. High level responses below; I attached our proposed redline that mirrors the exact language we typically accept for enterprise customers.

1) Training carve-out: Redwood can accept a strict \"no-training\" commitment for Customer Data as you defined (identifiable patient or business records). We propose the following narrowly scoped exception: Redwood may process pseudonymized, aggregated telemetry for service quality and reliability (e.g., latency, error rates, token distribution) provided such telemetry cannot be re-identified and is not retained beyond 90 days for training purposes. We will add an explicit prohibition on using Customer Data to update or fine-tune model weights absent written consent.

2) Subprocessors & transfers: attached is our current subprocessor matrix. For any subprocessor that will have access to plaintext Customer Data we commit to 30 days' notice and reasonable opportunity to object; for EU transfers we will execute the EU SCCs and provide a copy. We will also identify which subprocessors handle encryption keys vs. plaintext.

DocuSign: we are happy to execute via DocuSign. Once you confirm the redline you prefer, I'll route it through our Legal Ops and send a DocuSign envelope.

Couple of internal notes (CC'd Marcus and Neha) — please confirm whether the 90-day retention window for aggregated telemetry is acceptable or whether Acadia requires shorter retention. Also flag any subprocessors in the CSV that Acadia objects to so we can start remediation.

Best,
Jasmine Liu
Account Executive, Strategic Health
Redwood Inference

Attachment: Redwood_Acadia_DPA_proposed_20261123.pdf (redline)
Attachment: subprocessor-matrix-20261130.csv (updated)

From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>, Samantha Cole <samantha.cole@acadiahealth.com>
Date: Tue, 24 Nov 2026 15:47:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Acadia DPA questions — model training carve-out and subprocessors

Folks — quick legal confirm: the proposed language is acceptable from Redwood's perspective with two clarifications to avoid ambiguity in production:

• Definition of \"Customer Data\" — we'll adopt the redline's explicit definition (records tied to an identified or identifiable person or organization).
• \"Aggregated telemetry\" — define as metrics that have been pseudonymized and aggregated across customers such that no single customer's data can be isolated; retention capped at 90 days unless Customer explicitly consents to a longer window.

We will not accept any language that allows \"improvement\" in undefined terms; the clause must specifically restrict training/fine-tuning of model weights. I'll make those clarifying edits and upload an updated PDF. Once Acadia confirms, Legal Ops will generate the DocuSign envelope and include the executed SCCs as an exhibit for EU transfer compliance.

— Neha
Deputy GC, Redwood Inference

Attachment: Redwood_Acadia_DPA_proposed_20261124.pdf (clarified redline)

From: Joel Ramirez <joel.ramirez@acadiahealth.com>
To: Jasmine Liu <jasmine.liu@redwood.ai>, Samantha Cole <samantha.cole@acadiahealth.com>
Date: Thu, 03 Dec 2026 16:42:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Acadia DPA questions — model training carve-out and subprocessors

Jasmine / Team —

Thanks for the fast turnaround and for clarifying the points we raised. Acadia has reviewed the clarified redline and confirms the language meets our requirements (no training of Customer Data; aggregated telemetry as defined; 90-day retention acceptable). We also reviewed the subprocessor matrix and have two requests: (1) mark the encryption key manager as a restricted subprocessor (no access to plaintext without joint request), and (2) notify us with 30 days' notice for any future subprocessor that will have access to plaintext Customer Data.

I've routed the final DPA to our procurement team and signed in DocuSign — envelope link below. Please countersign at your earliest convenience so the PO can issue next week.

DocuSign envelope: https://docusign.net/externalSigning?doc=RDW-ACD-073

Regards,
Joel Ramirez
Sr. Manager, Vendor Operations
Acadia Health

Attachment: Executed_DPA_Acadia_20261203.pdf (executed copy via DocuSign)

-----Original Message-----
From: Acadia Health Legal <legal@acadiahealth.com>
Sent: Thu, 03 Dec 2026 15:10:00 -0800
To: jasmine.liu@redwood.ai
Subject: DocuSign: Acadia - Redwood DPA

(Procurement note: envelope contains redline accepted by both parties and SCC exhibit.)

title: [FinLytics] Inference endpoint outage — briefing & remediation commitments
preview:
From: Mark Robbins <mark@finlytics.com>
To: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.ai>, support@redwood.ai
Cc: Sara Li <sara.li@finlytics.com>
Date: 2026-12-03T08:12:00-08:00
Subject: Inference endpoint high latency / errors — urgent

Hi Mina / Redwood team,

We started seeing substantial latency and intermittent 5xx errors to our inference endpoint (model: rl-13b-response) beginning ~07:54 PST. Requests that normally return <150ms are taking 2–8s; ~12% of calls are returning 502/504. This is impacting live scoring for our production flows and causing transaction failures.

Can you confirm:
- Is this a known platform issue or something specific to our tenant?
- Do you have an estimate on time-to-recovery / mitigation steps?
- Any customer-facing mitigations we should apply on our side?

This is high priority for us — happy to hop on a bridge.

Thanks,
Mark Robbins
Director of Platform Engineering
FinLytics

Attachment: (none)


From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
To: Mark Robbins <mark@finlytics.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.ai>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-12-03T08:25:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Inference endpoint high latency / errors — urgent

Hi Mark — thanks for the report and sorry for the interruption. We're triaging now and I've escalated to on-call infra and model-serving teams.

Immediate actions:
- On-call engineer (Rishi) has been assigned and is collecting logs.
- We've opened an incident channel and will update within 30 minutes with initial findings.
- If you can share a short sample of request IDs or timestamps (if available), send them here and we’ll prioritize those traces.

We appreciate the heads up — will follow up shortly.

Best,
Monica Patel
Customer Success Lead, Redwood Inference

Attachment: (none)


From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
To: Mark Robbins <mark@finlytics.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-12-03T09:02:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Inference endpoint high latency / errors — urgent

Mark / team — quick technical update from the incident team.

What we're seeing:
- Impact window started 07:54 PST; increased queue times and 502/504 propagated when requests were routed to an overloaded GPU pool.
- Root cause (short term): an autoscaler regression introduced in yesterday's routing daemon (release v2.13.1) that caused stale routing entries for a subset of customer shards, which routed FinLytics traffic to a depleted pool.

Mitigation applied (09:00 PST):
- Manually rerouted FinLytics traffic away from the affected pool to a healthy pool.
- Rolled back the routing daemon to v2.13.0 for all edge nodes.

Current status (09:02 PST):
- Traffic is flowing through the healthy pool; error rate has dropped from ~12% to <1% and p95 latency is trending toward normal.

Next steps we’re scheduling:
- Collecting full request traces and KV cache snapshots for the affected interval.
- Postmortem/RCAs to be prepared and shared later today with timeline and action items.

We do not have any evidence of data exfiltration; logs indicate no unexpected exports.

If you have specific request ids / timestamps, please share them and we’ll attach the traces to the RCA.

— Rishi
Principal SRE, Redwood Inference

Attachment: (none)


From: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.ai>
To: Mark Robbins <mark@finlytics.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-12-03T11:15:00-08:00
Subject: FinLytics incident — RCA overview, timeline, and remediation commitments

Mark — thank you again for your patience this morning. Attached is our customer-facing RCA (redwood_rca.pdf) and an event timeline CSV (event-timeline-20261203.csv). Below is a concise summary of what happened, our remediation plan, and clear owner/ETA commitments.

Summary of impact
- Impact window: 2026-12-03 07:54 — 09:05 PST (approx).
- Symptoms: elevated p95 latency (up to 8s), ~12% 502/504 error rate at peak for FinLytics’ inference endpoint.
- Immediate customer impact: delayed scoring on live traffic; some transactions retried on your side.

Root cause (concise)
- A regression in the routing autoscaler (v2.13.1) introduced stale routing entries under a specific shard-count edge case. Those stale entries caused a portion of FinLytics’ traffic to be routed to a GPU pool that had exhausted pre-warmed capacity, resulting in queueing and timeouts.

Immediate mitigations performed
- 09:00 PST: Manual reroute of FinLytics traffic to a healthy GPU pool (temporary mitigation).
- 09:05 PST: Rollback of routing daemon to v2.13.0 across edge nodes.
- Ongoing: increased monitoring thresholds and manual watch until the long-term fix is deployed.

Short-term fixes (next 7 days) — owners & ETAs
- Hotfix to autoscaler to prevent stale entry creation: owner Rishi Malhotra — ETA 2026-12-06 17:00 PST.
- Add temporary guardrails in routing layer to prevent routing to pools with <X% available prewarmed slots: owner Ethan Park — ETA 2026-12-08 12:00 PST.
- Post-incident runbook update and customer playbook to notify customers earlier: owner Monica Patel — ETA 2026-12-10 18:00 PST.

Long-term mitigations (next 90 days)
- Autoscaler redesign to avoid single-release coupling with routing decisions (design & validation): owner Engineering — target Q1 2027.
- Add cross-tenant prewarm quotas and proactive pool rebalancing.

Customer commitments and next steps
- We will share a final, signed RCA (PDF) and raw timeline CSV by end of day today (attached).
- We propose a 60-minute technical review on 2026-12-06 (timezone options: 09:00 PST or 13:00 PST) to walk the timeline and answer questions. Please confirm preferred slot.
- As a goodwill gesture for the impact, we will apply a one-time service credit equivalent to two days of usage for the affected endpoints (will appear as a bill credit in your next invoice).

Security & data note
- We have no evidence of data exposure or unauthorized access during the incident. We will include artifact logs and sanitized traces in the detailed RCA package.

If you’d like a separate technical deep dive with our infra leads, we can follow the 60-minute review with an optional extended session.

Apologies again for the disruption — we take incidents like this seriously and will be working through the commitments above.

Best regards,

Mina Haddad

Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-12-03T09:02:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Inference endpoint high latency / errors — urgent

Mark / team — quick technical update from the incident team.

What we're seeing:
- Impact window started 07:54 PST; increased queue times and 502/504 propagated when requests were routed to an overloaded GPU pool.
- Root cause (short term): an autoscaler regression introduced in yesterday's routing daemon (release v2.13.1) that caused stale routing entries for a subset of customer shards, which routed FinLytics traffic to a depleted pool.

Mitigation applied (09:00 PST):
- Manually rerouted FinLytics traffic away from the affected pool to a healthy pool.
- Rolled back the routing daemon to v2.13.0 for all edge nodes.

Current status (09:02 PST):
- Traffic is flowing through the healthy pool; error rate has dropped from ~12% to <1% and p95 latency is trending toward normal.

Next steps we’re scheduling:
- Collecting full request traces and KV cache snapshots for the affected interval.
- Postmortem/RCAs to be prepared and shared later today with timeline and action items.

We do not have any evidence of data exfiltration; logs indicate no unexpected exports.

If you have specific request ids / timestamps, please share them and we’ll attach the traces to the RCA.

— Rishi
Principal SRE, Redwood Inference

Attachment: (none)


From: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.ai>
To: Mark Robbins <mark@finlytics.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-12-03T11:15:00-08:00
Subject: FinLytics incident — RCA overview, timeline, and remediation commitments

Mark — thank you again for your patience this morning. Attached is our customer-facing RCA (redwood_rca.pdf) and an event timeline CSV (event-timeline-20261203.csv). Below is a concise summary of what happened, our remediation plan, and clear owner/ETA commitments.

Summary of impact
- Impact window: 2026-12-03 07:54 — 09:05 PST (approx).
- Symptoms: elevated p95 latency (up to 8s), ~12% 502/504 error rate at peak for FinLytics’ inference endpoint.
- Immediate customer impact: delayed scoring on live traffic; some transactions retried on your side.

Root cause (concise)
- A regression in the routing autoscaler (v2.13.1) introduced stale routing entries under a specific shard-count edge case. Those stale entries caused a portion of FinLytics’ traffic to be routed to a GPU pool that had exhausted pre-warmed capacity, resulting in queueing and timeouts.

Immediate mitigations performed
- 09:00 PST: Manual reroute of FinLytics traffic to a healthy GPU pool (temporary mitigation).
- 09:05 PST: Rollback of routing daemon to v2.13.0 across edge nodes.
- Ongoing: increased monitoring thresholds and manual watch until the long-term fix is deployed.

Short-term fixes (next 7 days) — owners & ETAs
- Hotfix to autoscaler to prevent stale entry creation: owner Rishi Malhotra — ETA 2026-12-06 17:00 PST.
- Add temporary guardrails in routing layer to prevent routing to pools with <X% available prewarmed slots: owner Ethan Park — ETA 2026-12-08 12:00 PST.
- Post-incident runbook update and customer playbook to notify customers earlier: owner Monica Patel — ETA 2026-12-10 18:00 PST.

Long-term mitigations (next 90 days)
- Autoscaler redesign to avoid single-release coupling with routing decisions (design & validation): owner Engineering — target Q1 2027.
- Add cross-tenant prewarm quotas and proactive pool rebalancing.

Customer commitments and next steps
- We will share a final, signed RCA (PDF) and raw timeline CSV by end of day today (attached).
- We propose a 60-minute technical review on 2026-12-06 (timezone options: 09:00 PST or 13:00 PST) to walk the timeline and answer questions. Please confirm preferred slot.
- As a goodwill gesture for the impact, we will apply a one-time service credit equivalent to two days of usage for the affected endpoints (will appear as a bill credit in your next invoice).

Security & data note
- We have no evidence of data exposure or unauthorized access during the incident. We will include artifact logs and sanitized traces in the detailed RCA package.

If you’d like a separate technical deep dive with our infra leads, we can follow the 60-minute review with an optional extended session.

Apologies again for the disruption — we take incidents like this seriously and will be working through the commitments above.

Best regards,

Mina Haddad
Head of Customer Reliability, Redwood Inference
mina.haddad@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0142

Attachments: redwood_rca.pdf (customer-facing RCA), event-timeline-20261203.csv

title: QBR: technical debt impact & release schedule
preview:
From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-22T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: QBR: technical debt impact & release schedule

Team —

As part of the QBR packet I'm consolidating the product/eng section focused on: (1) where technical debt is materially changing our Nov/Dec release plan, (2) launch timeline adjustments we should surface to leadership, and (3) top delivery risks + mitigations. Attached are the current slide draft and the timeline workbook.

Immediate asks (can we get these by EOD Tuesday 24 Nov):
- Dylan: please add the top 3 tech-debt items that could shift release dates with estimated remediation effort and a short mitigation (1–2 lines).
- Ethan: confirm the rollout plan and call out any windows where infra patches block canary testing.
- Mina: note QA/load-test windows and any resourcing gaps (SDET/on-call).
- Aditya: update the risk score for the infra/ops items and recommend rollback windows.

I'm planning a short sync Wednesday 24 Nov 10:00 PST to review the consolidated slide. If you can't make it, please leave comments on the deck in Drive.

Attachments: qbr_slide_deck_v3.pdf (PDF, 1.2MB), release_timeline.xlsx (Spreadsheet, 48KB)

Thanks —
Lauren Bishop
Head of Product Operations
Redwood Inference


From: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-22T11:40:00-08:00
Subject: Re: QBR: technical debt impact & release schedule

Thanks Lauren — quick summary from infra/serving side.

On Mon, Nov 22, 2026 at 9:12 AM Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team —
> 
> As part of the QBR packet I'm consolidating the product/eng section focused on: (1) where technical debt is materially changing our Nov/Dec release plan, (2) launch timeline adjustments we should surface to leadership, and (3) top delivery risks + mitigations. Attached are the current slide draft and the timeline workbook.
> 
> Immediate asks (can we get these by EOD Tuesday 24 Nov):
> - Dylan: please add the top 3 tech-debt items that could shift release dates with estimated remediation effort and a short mitigation (1–2 lines).

My notes: top debt items that impact the release schedule
1) KV cache migration edge cases (INF-3472) — intermittent cache invalidation causing tail latency spikes under heavy streaming workloads. Remediation: patch to cache eviction logic + additional integration tests. Work estimate: 5 dev-days, 3 infra-days (can be done by mid-next sprint if prioritized). Risk: medium-high; direct effect on p95 for chat routes.
2) Attention-kernel fallback (runtime compatibility) — older CUDA kernels fail on particular model variants; current fallback path is untested at scale. Remediation: expand CI matrix, add canary on Dedicated pool. Work estimate: 7 dev-days + 2 GPU-days for validation. Risk: high if we expand model catalog before fix.
3) Telemetry sampling inconsistency — rollout of lower-sample telemetry reduced anomaly visibility, leading to slow detection windows. Remediation: revert sampling change for critical routes and add backfill job. Work estimate: 3 dev-days. Risk: medium.

Mitigations I recommend for the QBR deck: freeze non-critical refactors for the next two sprints, carve out a 48-hour validation window after infra patches, and schedule a limited canary (10% traffic) for model-variant launches.

I've attached an updated release_timeline.xlsx with suggested date shifts in the 'infra-impact' tab.

-Dylan
Principal Engineer, Serving Runtime
[Attachment: release_timeline.xlsx (Spreadsheet, 52KB)]


From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-23T10:05:00-08:00
Subject: Re: QBR: technical debt impact & release schedule

Adding rollout/QA perspective.

On 11/22/2026 11:40 AM, Dylan Brooks wrote:
> My notes: top debt items that impact the release schedule
> 1) KV cache migration edge cases (INF-3472) — intermittent cache invalidation causing tail latency spikes under heavy streaming workloads...

Two quick items from the release runbook side:
- If we accept Dylan's KV fix timeline (mid-next sprint), we should plan the canary to include a dedicated load test day two days before canary opens. That gives us a smoke+load profile representative of production.
- The attention-kernel fallback issue blocks a model-variant expansion; if we push that into the release window it will require an additional validation matrix and likely add 1 week to release if we do full-scale validation.

QA staffing: current QA roster covers functional tests but is thin on long-duration load and chaos testing. Recommend pulling 1 SDET from the infra SDET pool for 3 weeks (Nov 29–Dec 18) to support the validation plan. Cost: ~0.6 sprint of SDET time.

I've exported the tech-debt items into tech-debt-register.csv and attached — it contains estimated owner, days, and suggested priority.

Ethan
Director, Release Engineering
[Attachment: tech-debt-register.csv (CSV, 18KB)]


From: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-24T08:30:00-08:00
Subject: Re: QBR: technical debt impact & release schedule

Quick QA perspective and resourcing note.

Thanks for the summaries — conservative take from QA:
- We can accommodate the 48-hour validation window Dylan requested, but only if we lock the feature set for the canary 3 days prior. Any last-minute merges will require re-running the full validation matrix.
- Load testing: need the infra patch deployed to a staging cluster by Nov 30 to run the representative load profile on Dec 1–2. If that slips beyond Dec 3, we lose the pre-Canary validation window and should move the release out by one sprint.
- SDET support: I can second Ethan's ask — we will need a dedicated SDET for the duration (Nov 29–Dec 18). Also request backup on-call rotation for the week of Dec 6 (two people).

Suggested mitigation to include in QBR: prioritize KV cache patch and schedule the attention-kernel validation as a gated item before catalog expansion.

Redwood Inference

Subject: QBR: technical debt impact & release schedule

Team —

As part of the QBR packet I'm consolidating the product/eng section focused on: (1) where technical debt is materially changing our Nov/Dec release plan, (2) launch timeline adjustments we should surface to leadership, and (3) top delivery risks + mitigations. Attached are the current slide draft and the timeline workbook.

- Ethan: confirm the rollout plan and call out any windows where infra patches block canary testing.
- Mina: note QA/load-test windows and any resourcing gaps (SDET/on-call).
- Aditya: update the risk score for the infra/ops items and recommend rollback windows.

I'm planning a short sync Wednesday 24 Nov 10:00 PST to review the consolidated slide. If you can't make it, please leave comments on the deck in Drive.

Redwood Inference


From: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.com>
To: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>, Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-22T11:40:00-08:00
Subject: Re: QBR: technical debt impact & release schedule

Thanks Lauren — quick summary from infra/serving side.

On Mon, Nov 22, 2026 at 9:12 AM Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com> wrote:
> Team —
> 
> As part of the QBR packet I'm consolidating the product/eng section focused on: (1) where technical debt is materially changing our Nov/Dec release plan, (2) launch timeline adjustments we should surface to leadership, and (3) top delivery risks + mitigations. Attached are the current slide draft and the timeline workbook.
> 
> Immediate asks (can we get these by EOD Tuesday 24 Nov):
> - Dylan: please add the top 3 tech-debt items that could shift release dates with estimated remediation effort and a short mitigation (1–2 lines).

My notes: top debt items that impact the release schedule
1) KV cache migration edge cases (INF-3472) — intermittent cache invalidation causing tail latency spikes under heavy streaming workloads. Remediation: patch to cache eviction logic + additional integration tests. Work estimate: 5 dev-days, 3 infra-days (can be done by mid-next sprint if prioritized). Risk: medium-high; direct effect on p95 for chat routes.
2) Attention-kernel fallback (runtime compatibility) — older CUDA kernels fail on particular model variants; current fallback path is untested at scale. Remediation: expand CI matrix, add canary on Dedicated pool. Work estimate: 7 dev-days + 2 GPU-days for validation. Risk: high if we expand model catalog before fix.
3) Telemetry sampling inconsistency — rollout of lower-sample telemetry reduced anomaly visibility, leading to slow detection windows. Remediation: revert sampling change for critical routes and add backfill job. Work estimate: 3 dev-days. Risk: medium.

Mitigations I recommend for the QBR deck: freeze non-critical refactors for the next two sprints, carve out a 48-hour validation window after infra patches, and schedule a limited canary (10% traffic) for model-variant launches.

I've attached an updated release_timeline.xlsx with suggested date shifts in the 'infra-impact' tab.

-Dylan
Principal Engineer, Serving Runtime
[Attachment: release_timeline.xlsx (Spreadsheet, 52KB)]
