On 11/22/2026 11:40 AM, Dylan Brooks wrote:
> My notes: top debt items that impact the release schedule
> 1) KV cache migration edge cases (INF-3472) — intermittent cache invalidation causing tail latency spikes under heavy streaming workloads...

Two quick items from the release runbook side:
- If we accept Dylan's KV fix timeline (mid-next sprint), we should plan the canary to include a dedicated load test day two days before canary opens. That gives us a smoke+load profile representative of production.
- The attention-kernel fallback issue blocks a model-variant expansion; if we push that into the release window it will require an additional validation matrix and likely add 1 week to release if we do full-scale validation.

QA staffing: current QA roster covers functional tests but is thin on long-duration load and chaos testing. Recommend pulling 1 SDET from the infra SDET pool for 3 weeks (Nov 29–Dec 18) to support the validation plan. Cost: ~0.6 sprint of SDET time.

I've exported the tech-debt items into tech-debt-register.csv and attached — it contains estimated owner, days, and suggested priority.

Ethan
Director, Release Engineering

title: Re: Headcount rebalance for platform/runtime capacity
preview:
From: Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Dev Patel <dev@redwood.com>
Cc: Kelsey Shaw <kelsey@scaleops.com>
Date: Sat, 21 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Request: reallocate platform headcount to runtime infra

Hi Laura and Dev,

Short version: with sustained demand for low-latency inference from two enterprise customers (ACME Data and NorthStar), I propose we rebalance one open Platform SRE requisition and convert two near-term contractor slots to full-time roles focused on runtime/infra (inference kernel optimizations, batching, and reliability). This is intended to be budget-neutral within the Q4 headcount envelope.

Why now:
- ACME Data’s P0 workload requires consistent p95 latency under 80ms at 99k qps (their load ramp is scheduled Jan).
- Our current platform roadmap has slack in cross-regional SRE commitments for Q1 that can be shifted with minimal customer impact.

Proposal (details attached in headcount_rebalance_summary.pdf):
- Move one approved Platform SRE req (level E4) to a Runtime Infra Engineer (level E4) focused on inference serving.
- Convert 2 contractors (on-contract through Mar 2027) into FT Staff Engineers (level E5) to lead KV cache, batching, and quantization rollout.
- Use existing compensation bands; see compensation_grid_q4_2026.xlsx for band-level impact and suggested offer ranges.

Asks:
1) Laura: endorse operational/priority trade-offs for the SRE slot reallocation.
2) Dev: confirm this can be classified as cost-neutral for the purposes of headcount approval routing (we'd like to avoid an exec-level re-budget).
3) Marcus: can you pre-clear any finance constraints or required charge codes?

I’ve cc’d Kelsey from recruiting to flag the urgency so we can move candidates through offer if we get greenlight. I’ll attach a brief two-page summary and a spreadsheet with comp bands.

Thanks,
Ethan

--
Ethan Park
Director, Runtime Engineering
Redwood Inference


From: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.com>, Dev Patel <dev@redwood.com>
Cc: Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>, Kelsey Shaw <kelsey@scaleops.com>
Date: Sat, 21 Nov 2026 11:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: reallocate platform headcount to runtime infra

Ethan —

Thanks for the clear ask and the attached packet. I reviewed the summary and the proposed band moves. High level: I’m supportive of reassigning the open SRE req to runtime given the customer SLAs and low impact on our on-call coverage (I ran this by ops leads).

Notes / adjustments before final approval:
- Please include a prioritization note for the three hires (which one is first, which contractor conversion is tied to a specific project).
- Confirm the expected ramp timeline (start date targets) and any dependencies on infra capacity (GPU reservations / dedicated pools).
- Make sure recruiting has role-specific JD bullets for quantization experience and low-latency systems. Kelsey — can you create a short outbound checklist for those JDs?

If Ethan adds the prioritization + ramp dates, I can sign off at the hiring manager level and route to Dev/Marcus for finance and exec confirmation.

Laura

--
Laura Bennett
VP, Platform & Runtime
Redwood Inference


From: Dev Patel <dev@redwood.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus@redwood.com>
Cc: Kelsey Shaw <kelsey@scaleops.com>
Date: Sat, 21 Nov 2026 13:21:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: reallocate platform headcount to runtime infra — finance check required

Thanks all — I’m inclined to approve provided we meet two constraints:
1) Net headcount change to the org must be zero for the quarter (i.e., reassign an existing open req + contractor conversions with no new incremental budget beyond conversion uplift).
2) Compensation must remain within the published Q4 bands (DevOps/Infra band lines), or we need to escalate to People for exception.

Marcus — can you confirm that the conversion uplift (contractor to FT) is absorbed in the existing budget and whether we need to update the headcount plan code? If finance signs off, I’ll mark this as approved and request an expedited exec notification (we prefer not to go to the Exec Committee if finance confirms cost neutrality).

Ethan — please add the prioritization and target start dates Laura requested, and include final exhibit with offer ranges (use the comp grid).

Dev

--
Dev Patel
Chief Product & Platform Officer
Redwood Inference

title: [Urgent] VerdantPay: sustained P99 regressions + 429 amplification
preview:
From: Rajiv Menon <rajiv@verdantpay.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
Cc: Claire Liu <claire@verdantpay.com>
Date: 2026-11-18 08:12 -0800
Subject: Spike: P99 and request timeouts in production

Monica —

We started seeing user-visible timeouts and 429s again around 07:30 PT. Initial call-volume is within our usual range (no big traffic bursts on our side), but p95/p99 for text-generation calls jumped and some checkout flows are timing out. We are seeing many client retries (our SDK default retry). Can you confirm whether this is a configuration change on Redwood's side or something in the backend?

This is impacting three merchants; we need an ETA and immediate mitigation steps. I'm copying Claire and our SRE pager.

--
Rajiv Menon
Head of Platform
VerdantPay


From: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
To: Rajiv Menon <rajiv@verdantpay.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.ai>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.ai>, Claire Liu <claire@verdantpay.com>
Date: 2026-11-18 08:24 -0800
Subject: Re: Spike: P99 and request timeouts in production

Thanks Rajiv —

We’re on it. I’ve escalated to infra and the runtime oncall. Early hypothesis from the telemetry team: a rate-limit-induced retry storm is creating hotspots in a few serving pools, amplifying latency and producing cascading 429s/timeouts. Not yet confirmed, but that would explain client retries degenerating into overload.

Immediate steps we’ve kicked off:
- Rishi is pulling the last 6 hours of router + token-bucket metrics and prefix cache hit rates.
- Hannah is collecting span traces for affected requests and assembling a p95/p99 time-series (attaching an initial chart).
- Ethan will temporarily relax the strict global rate limiter for VerdantPay’s key (to see if that breaks the feedback loop) while we validate safety.

I’ll update in 30 mins. If we need executive visibility, please let me know and I’ll loop Legal/Revenue.

—
Monica Patel
Head of Customer Success, Redwood Inference


From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.ai>
To: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.ai>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.ai>, Rajiv Menon <rajiv@verdantpay.com>
Date: 2026-11-18 08:42 -0800
Subject: Re: Spike: P99 and request timeouts in production

Quick update —

Findings so far:
- Router logs show ~3x uptick in short-lived 429s across two hosted API clusters serving VerdantPay. These 429s align with client-side retries from VerdantPay SDK (observed via unique client_id header).
- Heatmap of active kernels indicates a few GPU shards being saturated by many short requests (high concurrency on small-context calls).
- KV cache hit-rate dropped by ~14% in the same window; prefix cache eviction patterns suggest repeated cold prefixes from retries.

Hypothesis: application-level retry loops are re-sending the same early tokens; rate limiting is kicking in and the resulting 429s are being retried, which pushes more requests into the scheduler; this increases tail latency and triggers timeouts further.

Action taken:
- Increased VerdantPay token bucket burst temporarily (15m) to break the immediate loop.
- Disabled one aggressive load-balancing optimization that was concentrating similar request keys onto the same shard while we investigate.

Next: collate request traces and share sanitized logs. ETA for initial log bundle: 20 mins.

-Rishi


From: Claire Liu <claire@verdantpay.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.ai>
Cc: Rajiv Menon <rajiv@verdantpay.com>, VerdantPay Ops <ops@verdantpay.com>
Date: 2026-11-18 09:05 -0800
Subject: Re: Spike: P99 and request timeouts in production

Thanks — grateful for the quick response. Two asks: 1) can you share the runbook steps you’re executing so we can mirror mitigations in our client (e.g., backoff tuning)? 2) any ETA for a durable fix so we’re not in a fragile state every time we have a small client retry storm?

We’ll temporarily disable client retries for non-idempotent endpoints and widen backoff while your team investigates.

-Claire


From: Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.ai>
To: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>, Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.ai>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.ai>
Cc: Rajiv Menon <rajiv@verdantpay.com>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-18 09:28 -0800
Subject: Re: Spike: P99 and request timeouts in production

Update from infra ops: the temporary burst increase is holding — we see the 429 rate drop by ~70% in the last 6 minutes and p95 has improved, but p99 still has intermittent spikes tied to a small subset of shards. I’ve attached traces and the latency chart.

Proposed next steps for stability:
1) Rolling change to scheduler to reduce same-key packing (30m rollout window, quick rollback).
2) Add a short (60s) client-scoped cooldown on identical request signatures at the edge to reduce duplicate in-flight work. This requires a small SDK-opt-in change (we can gate it to VerdantPay for now).
3) Medium-term: surface per-key retry amplification alerts and automated temporary burst increases for high-value customers.

If the execs want a short summary: impact, temporary mitigation, and roadmap. I can draft.

-Ethan


From: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
To: Rajiv Menon <rajiv@verdantpay.com>, Claire Liu <claire@verdantpay.com>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.ai>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.ai>, Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-19 17:33 -08:00
Subject: Re: Spike: P99 and request timeouts in production — exec brief attached

Rajiv / Claire —

Short exec summary attached (runbook + hypothesis + immediate fixes). TL;DR: transient retry amplification against rate limits created hotspots that increased tail latency. We implemented temporary burst increases, reduced same-key packing, and proposed edge-level duplicate suppression. Work items and owners are in the attached runbook.

We’ll follow up tomorrow with the post-mortem and a proposed SLA mitigation for VerdantPay while we deliver an automated guard against similar storms.

Attachments: verdantpay_latency_chart.png, request-trace-logs-20261118.zip, runbook_throttle_playbook.pdf

Best,
Monica Patel
Head of Customer Success
Redwood Inference

preview:
From: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.com>
To: Alex Chen <alex.chen@lumina.ai>

Date: Mon, 16 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Week 6 MAP check-in: acceptance runway, outstanding risks & owner roll-up

Hi Alex / Lumina team,

Quick pulse from our side for this week's Mutual Action Plan checkpoint (Week 6 of the POC). Attached is an updated MAP workbook (Lumina_MAP_v1.3.xlsx) and a draft acceptance criteria doc (acceptance_criteria_v2.md). Key items below — please confirm anything that needs to change before our touchpoint on Wednesday.

Top-level status:
- Technical integration: GREEN (model endpoints and prefix caching validated against sample dataset)
- Throughput: AMBER (observed 12% variance vs expected TPS during high-load replay — see notes)
- Acceptance runway: still aiming for go/no-go decision by 2026-11-24 (end of Week 7)

Action items for this week (owners):
- Marissa: finalize acceptance metric thresholds (latency p95, accuracy checks) — deliverable: acceptance_criteria_v2.md — due Tue 11/17
- Ethan: investigate replay variance and propose batching tweak (bench notes) — due Tue 11/17
- Sergio: prepare fallback routing plan for weekend demo (short doc) — due Wed 11/18
- Lumina: provide final list of 50 evaluation prompts and any PII redaction rules — owner: Alex / Procurement — due Tue 11/17

Meeting to discuss: Wednesday 11/18 10:00 PT — 30 minutes. Dial-in / recording will be shared; I recorded the short demo run earlier and attached as demo-recording-20261116.mp4.

Blockers we need Lumina help on:
- Confirmation on data retention window for logs (regulatory constraint?)
- Clarify whether production traffic will include multimodal metadata that changes token distributions (this impacts batching heuristics)

If anything here is off, flag it and we'll adjust. Thanks — looking forward to the sync.

Regards,
Dylan
Director, Solutions — Redwood Inference

Attachments: Lumina_MAP_v1.3.xlsx (spreadsheet), acceptance_criteria_v2.md (markdown), demo-recording-20261116.mp4 (video)


From: Alex Chen <alex.chen@lumina.ai>
To: Dylan Brooks <dylan.brooks@redwood.com>
Cc: Jordan Mills <jordan.mills@lumina.ai>, Procurement <procurement@lumina.ai>
Date: Mon, 16 Nov 2026 12:45:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Week 6 MAP check-in: acceptance runway, outstanding risks & owner roll-up

Thanks Dylan — appreciate the clear rundown and attachments. A few quick clarifications from our side before Wednesday: 

1) Evaluation prompts: we'll deliver the 50 prompts by EOD Tuesday (11/17). Expect some conversational prompts + system-level edge-cases.
2) Retention window: our security team requires logs retained for 90 days for the POC (can be shortened for demo but must be auditable). Is that compatible with Redwood's default telemetry?
3) Acceptance metric: on accuracy we need to distinguish between semantic-recall vs exact-match for a subset of prompts; can we record both?

Also, on the throughput variance — Jordan observed a spike when we replayed customer-like sessions with longer contexts (> 1,200 tokens). If batching reduces latency for shorter sessions but hurts tail latency for long-context sessions, we need to see the trade-offs.

We'll confirm owners internally (Jordan for logs & procurement) and join Wednesday.

Best,
Alex Chen
Head of ML Products — Lumina Analytics

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>

Cc: Alex Chen <alex.chen@lumina.ai>
Date: Tue, 17 Nov 2026 08:06:00 -08:00
Subject: Re: Week 6 MAP check-in: acceptance runway, outstanding risks & owner roll-up

Team — following up after review of the workbook and Alex's notes. I updated acceptance_criteria_v2.md with explicit thresholds and a short test plan. Summary of what's new: 

- Latency (p95): <= 420ms for short-context flows (<512 tokens); p95 for long-context flows (>=1200 tokens) must be documented and <= 1.8s for the POC acceptance tier.
- Semantic recall: measured via a 50-example human-eval rubric (score >= 0.78 weighted)
- Exact-match: secondary metric for deterministic prompts (threshold >= 0.65)
- Stability: <5% day-over-day variance in token cost and <10% variance in successful responses under a 1k-request replay

Action item updates (added owners & quick notes):
- Marissa (me): Deliver final acceptance doc and short test tracker — done (attached)
- Ethan: Bench replay — found that batching window of 20ms reduces average cost by ~9% but increases p95 for 1200+ token sessions by ~22% — working on adaptive batching suggestion for Wednesday
- Sergio: Fallback routing plan draft attached in the MAP workbook (sheet 'Fallback') — recommends warm standby smaller model for tail loads
- Dylan: coordinate decision runway and confirmation with Lumina procurement (you have the ownership)

Blockers for decision:
- Final confirmation on 90-day retention (Lumina) — if this is non-negotiable we'll need to enable extended trace retention and clarify cost impact
- Approval from Lumina security on the human-eval rubric (some prompts contain pseudo-PII examples)

I'll be on the call Wednesday and can present the acceptance thresholds. If Lumina wants to negotiate the p95 targets we should surface cost tradeoffs.

Thanks — Marissa
Sr. Product Manager, Redwood Inference

Attachments: acceptance_criteria_v2.md (final), MAP_change_log.txt

Hi Simon — great to meet earlier. Appreciate you and Lara taking the time. Short recap and one direct ask: are we fundamentally aligned enough to consider a short technical POC in the next 4–6 weeks?

Recap (brief):
- Goal: measure latency and token-cost on your short-doc extraction pipeline (~1k tokens avg).

preview:
From: Lena Ortiz <lena@northbridge.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.inference>
Cc: procurement@northbridge.com
Date: Tue, 3 Nov 2026 14:22:00 -0700
Subject: Scheduling evidence upload window and sample log questions

Hi Ethan,

Thanks for agreeing to help with Northbridge's SIG/SOC2 evidence collection. We need to schedule a secure upload window for logs and a couple of sample artifacts. Our security team wants the following clarified before we send anything: 

- Preferred transfer method (SFTP, secure Drive, or signed upload URL)
- Expected file naming convention and max file size
- Whether you need raw logs or a masked/redacted sample
- Retention window once we upload (for our records)

Can we aim for a transfer window next Tuesday (11/10) afternoon PST? If that doesn't work, propose your next best slot.

I'll loop in our security contact (security@northbridge.com) and procurement for timeline alignment.

Thanks,
Lena Ortiz
Senior Security Analyst
Northbridge Financial

--
Attachment: none


From: Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.inference>
To: Lena Ortiz <lena@northbridge.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.inference>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.inference>
Date: Tue, 3 Nov 2026 16:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Scheduling evidence upload window and sample log questions

Hi Lena —

Thanks, and great questions. Proposed answers and ops notes below so your security team can validate: 

1) Transfer method: we prefer a time-limited SFTP account for uploads (50GB limit by default). If your org requires cloud-native sharing we can accept a locked Google Drive folder with restricted access, or a pre-signed HTTPS upload link.

2) File naming: please include <company>_<env>_<artifact>_<YYYYMMDD>. E.g., northbridge_prod_app-logs_20261110.tar.gz. Max single-file size 10GB; we can accept chunked archives.

3) Redaction: we only need a redacted sample for initial review. For full audits we request raw logs but we can work with a hash/manifest + redacted sample first. We'll provide a short redaction guidance doc (PII patterns to remove) — see attachment we will send once we confirm the window.

4) Retention: upload artifacts in the secure bucket are retained for 30 days by default; if you need a shorter or longer window tell us and we can adjust for the intake. For SOC 2 evidence collection we typically snapshot and retain a copy for 90 days under a limited-access archive.

Proposed windows (PST):
- Tue 2026-11-10 13:00–15:00
- Wed 2026-11-11 09:00–11:00 (backup)

Action items for us: Ben will provision the SFTP account when you confirm the window. Marissa will attach the redaction guidance and a sample manifest.

Do either of those windows work?

Cheers,
Ethan Park
Security Programs Manager
Redwood Inference

--
Attachment: none

Hi Lena and team — following up with answers and the artifacts we'll provide before the upload window:

Hi Lena —

Thanks, and great questions. Proposed answers and ops notes below so your security team can validate: 

1) Transfer method: we prefer a time-limited SFTP account for uploads (50GB limit by default). If your org requires cloud-native sharing we can accept a locked Google Drive folder with restricted access, or a pre-signed HTTPS upload link.

2) File naming: please include <company>_<env>_<artifact>_<YYYYMMDD>. E.g., northbridge_prod_app-logs_20261110.tar.gz. Max single-file size 10GB; we can accept chunked archives.

3) Redaction: we only need a redacted sample for initial review. For full audits we request raw logs but we can work with a hash/manifest + redacted sample first. We'll provide a short redaction guidance doc (PII patterns to remove) — see attachment we will send once we confirm the window.

4) Retention: upload artifacts in the secure bucket are retained for 30 days by default; if you need a shorter or longer window tell us and we can adjust for the intake. For SOC 2 evidence collection we typically snapshot and retain a copy for 90 days under a limited-access archive.

Proposed windows (PST):
- Tue 2026-11-10 13:00–15:00
- Wed 2026-11-11 09:00–11:00 (backup)

Action items for us: Ben will provision the SFTP account when you confirm the window. Marissa will attach the redaction guidance and a sample manifest.

Do either of those windows work?

Cheers,
Ethan Park

Redwood Inference

Diego / Marissa,

Thanks for the quick sync last week. Our architects (Ayesha in Bangalore, Tom in Frankfurt, and myself in New York) are keen to do a 90–120 minute technical deep-dive next week to validate model routing, KV cache behavior, and secure egress patterns. We ran availability against our calendar and attached a short constraints sheet (vertex_constraints.xlsx).

High level asks:
- 90–120 minutes, prefer live run-through of a sample workload and Q&A with our infra team.
- We need a DST-safe option because our Frankfurt team has a partial-permanent flex schedule and daylight-savings transitions cause confusion when coordinating with APAC.
- Two fallback slots in case of last-minute travel.

Raw availability from our side (all local times):
- Ayesha (Bangalore): 09:00–11:30 IST or 18:00–20:00 IST (best window: 18:00 IST)
- Tom (Frankfurt, CET): 09:00–12:00 CET or 16:00–18:30 CET
- Martin (NYC, EST): 08:00–11:00 EST or 17:00–19:00 EST

We recognize there may be only small overlap. Can you propose 3 candidate windows (including at least one with short-hours overlap using an engineer rotation) and include a fallback single-region run if a full tri-region session is impossible?

Attachment: vertex_constraints.xlsx (spreadsheet with individual names and blocked times)

Cheers,
Martin Lowe
VP Solutions, VertexCloud
[m] +1 347 555 0198

title: After-hours: Layovers & driver surge plan — Greensboro roadshow (Nov 9)
preview:
From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: BlueLine Shuttles <ops@bluelineshuttles.com>
Date: Mon, 9 Nov 2026 20:13:00 -0500
Subject: Greensboro — flight cancellations causing layovers / quick surge plan

Team,

Two routing notes from the field: DL 447 (crew + demo kit) and AA 2215 (senior rep) are delayed — both now listed as overnight layovers in CLT which throws our shuttle windows off. We still have the majority of crew landing in GSO between 22:00–01:00, but there will be a cluster arriving after midnight and one small group rebooked into early AM.

Immediate asks:
- Can BlueLine provide 2 extra vehicles (minivan + 12-passenger) for midnight–02:30 to cover staggered arrivals?
- Marissa: can we hold 6 rooms under 'Redwood Roadshow' for late check-ins (arrivals up to 02:00)?
- Ethan/Laura: I need a short on-site coverage shuffle so late arrivals don't miss first morning shift (attach tentative manifest).

Attachments: field_manifest_GBO-11-09.csv (includes attendee flight numbers / preferred hotel)

I realize this is late; please reply with confirmations and driver contact numbers. If BlueLine can't stretch, we will look for local taxis but want to avoid multiple groups splitting equipment.

Thanks,
Connor
Sr. Field Ops Coordinator
Redwood Inference
+1 (415) 555-0198


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Hampton Inn Greensboro <gm@hampton-gbo.com>
Date: Mon, 9 Nov 2026 20:29:00 -0500
Subject: Re: Greensboro — flight cancellations causing layovers / quick surge plan

Connor —

I can hold 6 rooms at Hampton Inn Greensboro under the group name 'Redwood Roadshow' for late arrivals. They will allow express late check-in for us; I asked them to pre-authorize cards to avoid long desk lines. Voucher will be sent to the desk.

Hampton contact: Miguel Ramos (GM) — gm@hampton-gbo.com — +1 (336) 555-4302. If we need more than 6 rooms, they can block additional rooms at a corporate rate but need notice by 23:00.

I'll upload the hotel voucher as a pdf in a minute and loop in EventWorks if any equipment needs overnight storage at the venue.

-Marissa
Field Marketing Manager
Redwood Inference


From: BlueLine Shuttles <ops@bluelineshuttles.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 9 Nov 2026 21:02:00 -0500
Subject: Re: Greensboro — flight cancellations causing layovers / quick surge plan

Connor / Marissa —

We can do 1 minivan (5 pax + equipment) and 1 12-passenger van for the 23:30–02:30 window. Drivers: Luis Mendoza (cell +1 704-555-0109) and Tara Chen (cell +1 704-555-0115). Both have experience with overnight airport pickups and are aware of the flight delay codes.

Pickup plan:
- 23:30–00:15: CLT arrivals -> direct to Hampton Inn Greensboro (cluster 1, demo cases)
- 00:30–01:30: GSO late arrivals -> split by equipment needs (Luis takes equipment-heavy loads)
- 01:30–02:30: contingency runs for rebooked passengers to alternate hotels

Note: We'll bill per-trip at the previously agreed event rate; ETA for invoicing will be within 72 hours. Please confirm driver rendezvous point (ticketing vs baggage claim) and any special equipment loaders.

— BlueLine Ops
BlueLine Shuttles
ops@bluelineshuttles.com


From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 9 Nov 2026 22:05:00 -0500
Subject: Re: Greensboro — flight cancellations causing layovers / quick surge plan

Got it — thanks. A couple of operational notes:

1) Shift adjustments: I can cover the 07:00–11:00 morning demo shift on-site; please move Ash (overnight arrival) to the 11:00–15:00 slot so we avoid overlap while he clears customs/immigration.
2) Equipment custody: If Luis will take equipment-heavy loads, he'll need a second set of keys and the crate inventory sheet signed.
3) Per diem / cash: We have 2 international crew arriving with longer rebook windows — Laura, can you bring petty cash ($150 total) to the hotel front desk by 01:00 for overnight meal cover?

I attached a revised pickup-to-shift mapping in driver_manifest_v2.xlsx.

Ethan
Field Ops Lead
Redwood Inference


From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
To: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: BlueLine Shuttles <ops@bluelineshuttles.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 00:46:00 -0500
Subject: Fwd: Greensboro pickup windows + manifest (final)

Forwarding updated manifest and pickup windows — please use this as the authoritative list for drivers.

--- Forwarded message ---
From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 00:39:00 -0500
Subject: Greensboro pickup windows + manifest (final)

Pickup windows (local times):
- 23:40 Arrivals (DL 447 alt routing) — Luis (minivan) to Hampton Inn GBO — note: 3 crates + 2 suitcases
- 00:50 GSO domestic cluster — Tara (12pax) to Hampton Inn GBO — note: 6 pax, small bag only
- 01:55 contingency — on-call driver (TBD) for rebook + alternate hotel drop

Attachments: driver_manifest_v2.xlsx, hotel_voucher_GBO.pdf

Laura
Field Logistics Specialist
Redwood Inference
+1 (212) 555-0182


From: Connor O'Brien <connor.obrien@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: BlueLine Shuttles <ops@bluelineshuttles.com>, Hampton Inn Greensboro <gm@hampton-gbo.com>, EventWorks Rentals <katie@eventworks.co>
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 02:47:00 -0500
Subject: Re: Greensboro — confirmed (drivers, rooms, on-site shuffles)

All —

Summary / action items (final):
- BlueLine confirmed: Luis Mendoza (+1 704-555-0109) and Tara Chen (+1 704-555-0115) on standby 23:30–02:30. Luis handles crates.
- Hampton Inn: 6 rooms held under 'Redwood Roadshow'; Miguel Ramos to accept hotel_voucher_GBO.pdf at front desk; extra rooms if needed by 23:00.
- Inventory: Laura to hand Luis the crate inventory and two spare keys at pickup.
- Shift swaps: Ethan will cover 07:00–11:00; Ash moved to 11:00–15:00 (Ethan to confirm on arrival).
- Per diem: Laura to deliver petty cash ($150) to the front desk by 01:00.
- EventWorks: Katie (cc'd) will store overnight risers at the loading dock if we need to stage demo pieces.

Please confirm receipt. If anything changes (more rebooks, lost luggage), reply ALL and call Luis directly. Appreciate the quick responses tonight — let's keep lines tight.

Connor
Sr. Field Ops Coordinator
Redwood Inference
Phone: +1 (415) 555-0198

title: Pivoting scheduled demo into hands-on engineering lab
preview:
From: Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Rivers <alex@stratus.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-06T09:14:00-08:00
Subject: Pivoting our Tue demo (Nov 10) into a hands-on engineering lab

Hi Alex —

Quick update on our session scheduled for Tue (Nov 10) at 10:00 PT. Stratus mentioned they want a deeper technical run-through focused on integration patterns and a short POC scoping block. Given that ask, we think it makes more sense to convert the 45-minute overview into a 90-minute hands-on engineering lab so the platform engineers can try a short integration path and we can capture concrete next steps.

Two constraints on our side: I’m traveling back to the East Coast the morning of the 10th and my calendar will be fragmented, and Karthik (lead solutions engineer) is only available in the morning PT windows on Nov 12–13. Monica is happy to join as well.

Proposed options — please tell us which works for you (or suggest slots):
- Option A: Wed Nov 11, 09:00–10:30 PT — hands-on lab + POC scoping
- Option B: Thu Nov 12, 08:30–10:00 PT — slightly earlier for Karthik
- Option C: Fri Nov 13, 11:00–12:30 PT — if you need a later slot

I attached a one-page agenda and a draft lab outline so you can share with your engineers. If you’d prefer to keep it as a short overview, we can keep Tue’s slot and follow up with a separate lab — want to be flexible.

Next steps: tell us preferred option and whether your engineers will attend with admin access (we’ll provide temporary keys/sandbox).

Thanks,
Ethan Park
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ethan@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0123
Redwood Inference

Attachments: redwood-onepager.pdf (1 page), redwood-hands-on-agenda-v1.md (draft agenda)

From: Alex Rivers <alex@stratus.ai>
To: Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.ai>
Cc: partnerships@stratus.ai
Date: 2026-11-06T11:07:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Pivoting our Tue demo (Nov 10) into a hands-on engineering lab

Thanks Ethan — appreciate the heads up. Our CTO and two engineers asked specifically for a hands-on session so your call to expand to a lab makes sense.

We have these windows open on our side (PST):
- Wed Nov 11: 09:00–11:00 OK
- Thu Nov 12: 08:00–09:30 OK (might be tight)
- Fri Nov 13: 10:30–12:30 OK

Option A (Wed 11 09:00–10:30) looks the cleanest for us. We’ll invite the engineers and confirm if they need admin/sandbox access — likely yes for one of them.

Quick Q: will you still cover the high-level business outcomes at the start, or should we assume attendees are technical-only and skip that?

—Alex
Alex Rivers
Director of Engineering | Stratus AI
alex@stratus.ai

> Quick update on our session scheduled for Tue (Nov 10) at 10:00 PT. Stratus mentioned they want a deeper technical run-through...

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
To: Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.ai>, Alex Rivers <alex@stratus.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-07T07:22:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Pivoting our Tue demo (Nov 10) into a hands-on engineering lab

Ethan / Alex —

I can run the lab on Wed Nov 11 at 09:00 PT. Outline I suggest (attached agenda v1):
- 09:00–09:10: Quick business framing (Ethan) + goal alignment
- 09:10–09:35: Live walk-through of integration (Karthik) — code snippets and infra map
- 09:35–10:10: Hands-on exercise for Stratus engineers (sandbox + sample repo)
- 10:10–10:30: POC scoping & next steps (action owners, success metrics)

Requests before the session:
- Please share one or two sample use cases you want to test in the lab (Slack or email)
- Grant one engineer temporary sandbox access (we’ll rotate keys)

I’m attaching the draft agenda and a short runbook for the sandbox setup. I’ll create a calendar invite for Wed 09:00–10:30 PT once Alex confirms.

Karthik Iyer
Lead Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
karthik@redwood.ai

Attachments: redwood-hands-on-agenda-v1.md, sandbox-runbook.txt

From: Alex Rivers <alex@stratus.ai>
To: Ethan Park <ethan@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica@redwood.ai>, partnerships@stratus.ai
Date: 2026-11-09T16:02:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Pivoting our Tue demo (Nov 10) into a hands-on engineering lab

Confirmed — Wed Nov 11 09:00–10:30 PT works for our team. I’ve invited the CTO and two engineers; one will need sandbox access. I’ll send their emails in a follow-up so you can provision temporary creds.

Small ask: please include a 5–10 minute section at the top that summarizes potential cost/latency tradeoffs for different deployment modes (hosted vs. dedicated). Our CTO wants that context before we dive into infra.

I also added the HubSpot deal ID (D-78341) to our record and attached the calendar invite. Looking forward to it.

—Alex

> I can run the lab on Wed Nov 11 at 09:00 PT. Outline I suggest (attached agenda v1)...

title: Payee profile consolidation & wire instruction update
preview:
From: Lance Winters <lance.winters@bluepine-supplies.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
Cc: vendors@bluepine.com
Date: Tue, 3 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Request: Consolidation of payee record + new wire instructions (PO 5879)

Hi Ethan,

We are consolidating two legal entities (BluePine Supplies LLC and BluePine Industrial LLC) into one remit-to. Can you update Redwood’s vendor master so future payments route to the consolidated entity?

Key items:
- New legal name: BluePine Supplies, EIN 12-3456789
- New remit address: 831 Harbor Lane, Suite 200, Portland, OR 97205
- New bank for wires: Pacific Coast Bank, ABA 121000358, acct xxxxxx2345 (see attached bank instruction PDF)
- PO affected: PO-5879 (please route to wire for the invoice attached to this PO)

Attached: BluePine_Wire_Instructions_20261103.pdf (bank letter on letterhead). I can also provide a vendor-signed W-9 if needed — finance can be looped in at ap@bluepine-supplies.com.

Let me know next steps and any forms Redwood needs.

Regards,
Lance Winters
Director, Finance Operations
BluePine Supplies

---

From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
To: Lance Winters <lance.winters@bluepine-supplies.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 3 Nov 2026 10:05:12 -0800
Subject: Re: Request: Consolidation of payee record + new wire instructions (PO 5879)

Subject: Vendor tax preboarding — EIN validation & procurement gate

Hi Sophie —

Thanks for agreeing to fast-track setup with Procurement. A few items we need to clear the PO gate so AP can push the first payment over ACH: 

1) Completed W-9 (PDF). We need the nine-digit EIN to match our vendor record.
2) Proof of EIN: either an IRS 147-C letter or a bank letter that shows the legal name + EIN (bank letter redacted is fine).
3) Signed supplier onboarding form (I attached a blank copy below) — needs an authorized signatory.
4) Preferred ACH routing instructions (bank name + last 4 digits; full account via secure portal later).

If you can reply with the W-9 and either the 147-C or a bank letter, we can move to PO issuance. If there's anything blocking the 147-C, a notarized affidavit plus a firm timeline for the IRS letter can be an acceptable interim.

Next steps / timeline suggestion:
- Sophie: send documents by end of day Monday (11/2)
- Monica: procurement validation and escalate to AP once docs verified (target 11/3)
- Ethan: confirm PO creation and share PO number

We use DocuSign for our onboarding form; if you prefer to sign offline and upload a PDF that's fine too.

Thanks — appreciate the quick turnaround.

title: VPC pricing construct: amortize platform fee vs. operator credits
preview:
From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
To: HelioHealth Procurement <procurement@heliohealth.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-14T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: VPC pricing construct: amortize platform fee vs. operator credits

Hi Iris / procurement team,

Thanks again for the time this morning. Per our discussion, I wanted to surface two alternative commercial constructs for the Private (VPC) deployment we scoped:

Option A — Amortized platform fee (recommended if you want predictable OPEX):
- One-time platform activation fee ($180k) amortized over 24 months in monthly invoices
- Operator licenses included (up to 8 operators). Additional operator seat $2,500/seat/mo
- 12-month support Tier 2 included (response SLA: 4h P1)

Option B — Lower platform fee + migration credits (recommended if you want lower upfront):
- Reduced platform activation fee ($90k) + migration credit of $70k applied to first 6 invoices
- Per-node entitlement (anchored to 6 committed nodes), burst capacity billed per GPU-hour
- Support add-on: Tier 2 or Tier 3 available for uplift

Key trade-offs we discussed: amortization buys you stable monthly cost and includes on-call support; node-entitlement gives lower upfront but can vary month-to-month. We also reviewed a carveout for telemetry retention (HelioHealth requested 24 months) which adds a $12k/year observability addon unless covered by a longer platform amortization.

Next asks from us:
1) Which construct is preferred (A or B)?
2) Are the operator count and telemetry retention acceptable as outlined?
3) Will your procurement team need a version of the SOW with payment milestones (we can produce an exhibit that amortizes the platform fee)?

I attached the draft commercial offer and a single-page comparison table for both options. If helpful, Marissa can walk through numbers in a short call early next week.

Regards,
Ethan Park
Senior Account Executive
Redwood Inference

Attachment: HelioHealth-Commercial-Offer-v2.pdf (PDF, 412 KB)


From: Iris Nolan <iris.nolan@heliohealth.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
Cc: HelioHealth Procurement <procurement@heliohealth.com>
Date: 2026-10-15T11:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: VPC pricing construct: amortize platform fee vs. operator credits

Ethan —

Thanks for the clear breakdown. A few clarifying points from procurement:
- HelioHealth prefers lower upfront CAPEX for Q4, so Option B is attractive. However we need a firm maximum monthly cap for burst to avoid surprises. Can you provide an example month with 30% burst above committed nodes and the estimated GPU-hour bill?
- On operator licenses: our security team wants a narrow list (SRE + 1 integrator). If we commit to 4 operator seats, can we reserve two additional seats for short-term contractors (on a billed-as-used basis)?
- The telemetry retention add-on will likely be required at 24 months. We need to understand exact cost drivers.

If you can update the offer to show: (1) committed-node + 30% burst cost example, (2) operator seat flexible add-on language, and (3) a short SOW payment exhibit tagging the migration credit against the first 6 invoices, we can move fast.

Appreciate it —
Iris
Head of Procurement, HelioHealth

Quoted text: "On 2026-10-14, Ethan Park wrote: ..."


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-16T08:29:00-07:00
Subject: Re: VPC pricing construct: amortize platform fee vs. operator credits — internal notes

Ethan / Priyom,

Notes after Iris' ask:
- Burst example: assume committed 6 nodes (each node ~= 1x A100 equiv for Helio). 30% burst ~= +2 nodes for peak month. Our burst billing rate we used in prior offers: $6.5 / GPU-hour. Example month calc (30-day month, 24h):
  - Committed: 6 nodes * 24h * 30d * $4.2/hr (committed rate) = $181,440
  - Burst: 2 nodes * 24h * 30d * $6.5/hr = $93,600
  - Total month = $275,040 (this is extreme; typical production months will have lower active fraction).

Subject: VPC pricing construct: amortize platform fee vs. operator credits

Hi Iris / procurement team,

Thanks again for the time this morning. Per our discussion, I wanted to surface two alternative commercial constructs for the Private (VPC) deployment we scoped:

Option A — Amortized platform fee (recommended if you want predictable OPEX):
- One-time platform activation fee ($180k) amortized over 24 months in monthly invoices
- Operator licenses included (up to 8 operators). Additional operator seat $2,500/seat/mo
- 12-month support Tier 2 included (response SLA: 4h P1)

Option B — Lower platform fee + migration credits (recommended if you want lower upfront):
- Reduced platform activation fee ($90k) + migration credit of $70k applied to first 6 invoices
- Per-node entitlement (anchored to 6 committed nodes), burst capacity billed per GPU-hour
- Support add-on: Tier 2 or Tier 3 available for uplift

Key trade-offs we discussed: amortization buys you stable monthly cost and includes on-call support; node-entitlement gives lower upfront but can vary month-to-month. We also reviewed a carveout for telemetry retention (HelioHealth requested 24 months) which adds a $12k/year observability addon unless covered by a longer platform amortization.

Next asks from us:
1) Which construct is preferred (A or B)?
2) Are the operator count and telemetry retention acceptable as outlined?
3) Will your procurement team need a version of the SOW with payment milestones (we can produce an exhibit that amortizes the platform fee)?

title: Highline Health: Incident update and next steps (data access latency)
preview:
From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
To: On-call <oncall@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 12 Oct 2026 08:14:00 -0700
Subject: [INCIDENT] Elevated inference latency affecting Highline Health - initial alert

Team,

We detected a sustained spike in tail latency for text-generation requests routed to model-redwood/llama-2-70b-quantized in us-west-2 starting ~08:05 PDT. Symptoms: 95th/99th latencies > 5s, increased HTTP 502s for multi-turn requests. Affected tenant: Highline Health (production).

Actions taken so far:
- Opened incident INF-4821 and created a Slack bridge: #incident-inf-4821 (link below).
- Routed new traffic to model-redwood/llama-2-70b-quantized-fallback (lower perf but stable).
- Captured KV cache and runtime logs (see bridge-log-excerpt.txt).
- Notified CS and the Highline primary contact (Julia Sullivan).

Immediate asks:
- Monica: if safe, initiate rollback to last-known-good kernel build (rollback candidate: kernel-11b-20261008) and verify canary in 10m.
- Ethan: validate KV cache health and look for cache invalidations / evictions around 08:00-08:10.
- Hannah: draft a 1-paragraph customer-facing status we can send within 20 minutes (concise, ETA for restoration, remediation actions).

I'll coordinate communications and update the ticket. Attach: bridge-log-excerpt.txt.

Rishi
Director, Customer Reliability
Redwood Inference

References: https://status.redwood.ai/incident/2026-10-12-highline | JIRA: INF-4821


From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, On-call <oncall@redwood.ai>
Cc: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 12 Oct 2026 08:24:00 -0700
Subject: Re: [INCIDENT] Elevated inference latency affecting Highline Health - initial alert

On Mon, Oct 12, 2026 at 08:14 AM Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai> wrote:
> (initial alert)

Started the canary rollback to kernel-11b-20261008 on a small pool (3 nodes) at 08:22. Canary metrics show 95th latency back to ~280ms after warm-up. Rolling to 25% of traffic now; will monitor for 15m before full rollout.

If the convoy looks good, we should be able to return to normal routing within ~30-45m. Will post results to the bridge.

-Monica
Senior SRE

Attachment: none (see bridge logs for traces)


From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
To: Julia Sullivan <julia.sullivan@highlinehealth.org>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 12 Oct 2026 08:35:00 -0700
Subject: Highline Health — Service status & next steps (latency impact)

Julia —

Short update from Redwood: we are actively investigating elevated inference latency affecting some production requests for Highline Health. Symptoms were detected at ~08:05 PDT. Our engineers have isolated a kernel-level regression that, when combined with a recent cache invalidation, caused tail-latency spikes.

What we have done so far:
- Switched new requests to a validated fallback model variant to reduce error rates.
- Initiated a controlled rollback of the kernel to a previously verified build; canary shows latency improving.
- Opened a full incident (INF-4821) and engaged engineering, SRE, and CS.

Expected timeline:
- Canary rollback in progress now; we expect restoration of normal latency within 30–60 minutes if metrics continue to improve.

What you should expect from us:
- Real-time updates in this thread and the status page (https://status.redwood.ai/incident/2026-10-12-highline).
- A follow-up executive summary and a draft postmortem within 48 hours.
- If you’d like, we will schedule a brief 30-minute call to walk through impact and mitigation.

Apologies for the disruption — we know this affects live traffic, and we’re prioritizing a fast, safe recovery. I’ll update you again within 30 minutes or as the rollback completes.

Attachment: Customer-Status-Summary-20261012.pdf

Best,
Rishi Malhotra
Director, Customer Reliability
Redwood Inference


From: Julia Sullivan <julia.sullivan@highlinehealth.org>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
Cc: Highline Health Support <support@highlinehealth.org>, Tom Erickson <tom.erickson@highlinehealth.org>
Date: Mon, 12 Oct 2026 09:01:12 -0700
Subject: Re: Highline Health — Service status & next steps (latency impact)

On Mon, Oct 12, 2026 at 08:35 AM Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai> wrote:
> Short update from Redwood...

Thanks for the quick update, Rishi. Two asks from Highline: 1) Please confirm whether any customer data was exposed or persisted outside of our configured retention window during this incident. 2) Please include our security contact on the postmortem when available.

We appreciate the proactive routing to the fallback model. We’ll stand by for confirmation of full restoration and the postmortem meeting invite.

—Julia
Head of Platform Ops, Highline Health


From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
To: Julia Sullivan <julia.sullivan@highlinehealth.org>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>, Highline Security <security@highlinehealth.org>
Date: Mon, 12 Oct 2026 13:58:00 -0700
Subject: Incident INF-4821 — restore confirmed; initial RCA & proposed next steps

Julia / Highline team —

Service was restored to normal routing at 13:46 PDT after a full rollout of kernel-11b-20261008 and targeted KV cache warm-up. Key points:

- Impact window: 08:05–13:46 PDT. During that window, customers experienced elevated tail latency and intermittent 502s for multi-turn requests.
- Root cause (initial): a staged kernel change combined with an unexpected KV cache invalidation created a hot-key condition that amplified a scheduling regression in the new kernel. The combination pushed tail latencies above our SLO.
- Data exposure: preliminary analysis shows no evidence of unauthorized data access or persistence beyond configured retention. We will provide formal verification from our security team in the postmortem.

Attachments and links: draft RCA (root_cause-draft.pdf), bridge logs (bridge-log-excerpt.txt), status page: https://status.redwood.ai/incident/2026-10-12-highline.

Proposed next steps (owner / ETA):
- Full postmortem draft and security verification — Hannah Schmitt / 48 hours.
- Permanent mitigation (kernel hardening + additional cache safeguards) — Monica Patel / 2 weeks (patch + staged rollout).
- Credits & commercial: Redwood CS to propose a service credit for the impacted window — Grace O'Connor / 3 business days.
- Postmortem review meeting: Thu, Oct 14, 2026 10:00–10:30 PDT (video link to follow).

We understand this impacted production and will prioritize transparency and corrective action. Please let me know if you need additional information before the postmortem.

Regards,
Rishi

Reference: JIRA INF-4821 | Postmortem draft: https://drive.google.com/open?id=postmortem-Highline-2026-10-12

Agenda for Thu Oct 14, 10:00–10:30 PDT:
1) Timeline recap (5m) — Rishi
2) Technical root cause & remediation plan (10m) — Monica
3) Security verification & data-retention statement (5m) — Ethan
4) Commercial remediation (service credit) & next steps (5m) — Grace

- Kernel hardening patch & rollout plan (Monica) — due 2 weeks (Oct 26)

text:
From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
To: On-call <oncall@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 12 Oct 2026 08:14:00 -0700
Subject: [INCIDENT] Elevated inference latency affecting Highline Health - initial alert

Team,

We detected a sustained spike in tail latency for text-generation requests routed to model-redwood/llama-2-70b-quantized in us-west-2 starting ~08:05 PDT. Symptoms: 95th/99th latencies > 5s, increased HTTP 502s for multi-turn requests. Affected tenant: Highline Health (production).

Actions taken so far:
- Opened incident INF-4821 and created a Slack bridge: #incident-inf-4821 (link below).
- Routed new traffic to model-redwood/llama-2-70b-quantized-fallback (lower perf but stable).
- Captured KV cache and runtime logs (see bridge-log-excerpt.txt).
- Notified CS and the Highline primary contact (Julia Sullivan).

Immediate asks:
- Monica: if safe, initiate rollback to last-known-good kernel build (rollback candidate: kernel-11b-20261008) and verify canary in 10m.
- Ethan: validate KV cache health and look for cache invalidations / evictions around 08:00-08:10.
- Hannah: draft a 1-paragraph customer-facing status we can send within 20 minutes (concise, ETA for restoration, remediation actions).

I'll coordinate communications and update the ticket. Attach: bridge-log-excerpt.txt.

Rishi
Director, Customer Reliability
Redwood Inference

References: https://status.redwood.ai/incident/2026-10-12-highline | JIRA: INF-4821


From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>, On-call <oncall@redwood.ai>
Cc: Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 12 Oct 2026 08:24:00 -0700
Subject: Re: [INCIDENT] Elevated inference latency affecting Highline Health - initial alert

On Mon, Oct 12, 2026 at 08:14 AM Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai> wrote:
> (initial alert)

Started the canary rollback to kernel-11b-20261008 on a small pool (3 nodes) at 08:22. Canary metrics show 95th latency back to ~280ms after warm-up. Rolling to 25% of traffic now; will monitor for 15m before full rollout.

If the convoy looks good, we should be able to return to normal routing within ~30-45m. Will post results to the bridge.

-Monica
Senior SRE

Attachment: none (see bridge logs for traces)


From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
To: Julia Sullivan <julia.sullivan@highlinehealth.org>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>, Hannah Schmitt <hannah.schmitt@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>, Grace O'Connor <grace.oconnor@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 12 Oct 2026 08:35:00 -0700
Subject: Highline Health — Service status & next steps (latency impact)

Julia —

Short update from Redwood: we are actively investigating elevated inference latency affecting some production requests for Highline Health. Symptoms were detected at ~08:05 PDT. Our engineers have isolated a kernel-level regression that, when combined with a recent cache invalidation, caused tail-latency spikes.

What we have done so far:
- Switched new requests to a validated fallback model variant to reduce error rates.
- Initiated a controlled rollback of the kernel to a previously verified build; canary shows latency improving.
- Opened a full incident (INF-4821) and engaged engineering, SRE, and CS.

Expected timeline:
- Canary rollback in progress now; we expect restoration of normal latency within 30–60 minutes if metrics continue to improve.

What you should expect from us:
- Real-time updates in this thread and the status page (https://status.redwood.ai/incident/2026-10-12-highline).
- A follow-up executive summary and a draft postmortem within 48 hours.
- If you’d like, we will schedule a brief 30-minute call to walk through impact and mitigation.

Apologies for the disruption — we know this affects live traffic, and we’re prioritizing a fast, safe recovery. I’ll update you again within 30 minutes or as the rollback completes.

Attachment: Customer-Status-Summary-20261012.pdf

Best,
Rishi Malhotra
Director, Customer Reliability
Redwood Inference

title: Audit window mismatch — request interim attestation & bridge letter to unblock FirstBank procurement
preview:
From: Sarah Keller <sarah.keller@firstbankcorp.com>
To: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-10-11T09:15:00-07:00
Subject: SOC 2 coverage dates for procurement

Hi Vanessa,

Thanks for the demo last week — the team was impressed. Procurement has one final gating item: our vendor policy requires a SOC 2 Type II report covering a 12-month period that includes the fiscal year ending 2025 (i.e., audit period must include 2025-01-01 through 2025-12-31). I pulled the vendor portal and your most recent Type II appears to cover 2026-01-01 through 2026-12-31.

Can you confirm whether Redwood can provide either: 1) a SOC 2 Type II for the 2025 audit window, or 2) a formal bridge/interim attestation that maps controls from your current report back to our required period? Procurement needs this documented to move the contract to legal/PO.

We are on a tight deadline to hit FirstBank year-end procurement approvals, so any expedited path would be appreciated. If helpful, we can accept a lawyer-signed bridge letter plus a short evidence pack while you finalize the underlying audit.

Thanks,
Sarah
Sarah Keller
Vendor Risk Manager — FirstBank

Attachment: SOC2_request_form.pdf (stub)

From: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-10-11T10:03:00-07:00
Subject: Fwd: SOC 2 coverage dates for procurement — FirstBank (urgent)

Team — forwarding FirstBank's note (below). They will not accept our current report window and asked explicitly for either a 2025-period Type II or a bridge/interim attestation plus supporting evidence.

Impact: This is a $650k ARR expansion renewal with procurement timeline tied to their fiscal cutover. If we can't provide an acceptable attestation this week they will push the PO into next quarter, which risks losing the negotiated pricing and delaying Dedicated capacity provisioning.

Request: Ethan/Naomi — can you confirm feasibility of: 1) providing a lawyer-signed bridge letter attesting that key control mappings are unchanged between the 2025 and 2026 windows, and 2) bundling a minimal evidence set (policy snapshots, SOC scope diagram, control owner attestations) for procurement review? Vivek/Marissa — please advise on commercial mitigations we can offer (short credit, SLA addendum) to keep the deal on track while the letter is finalized.

I'm marking this as high priority — loop in whoever else you need. I'll be available all day for quick calls.

Thanks,
Vanessa Ortiz
VP, Enterprise Sales — Redwood Inference

--- Forwarded message from Sarah Keller (see above) ---

From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>
To: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-10-11T11:22:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: SOC 2 coverage dates for procurement — FirstBank (urgent)

Thanks Vanessa — tagging this urgent. Quick summary of options after a rapid check with our compliance partner and external auditors:

- Full retro SOC 2 Type II for 2025: NOT feasible within the timeline (would require re-running the audit and auditor availability into Q1).
- Bridge/interim attestation: Feasible. We can produce a controls bridge letter signed by Legal + our auditor letterhead that states: control design and operating effectiveness materially unchanged across the adjacent periods, documented mapping of relevant controls, and an index with minimal supporting artifacts.

What I need from Sales to scope the evidence pack:
1) Which controls are the customer most concerned about (if Procurement can flag high-priority controls: access mgmt, encryption, change mgmt, incident mgmt).
2) Confirmation that procurement will accept a bridge letter plus evidence instead of the full report (procurement already hinted they would accept that).

I'll draft an internal evidence checklist and a bridge letter template, then loop Legal for sign-off. ETA for a first draft: within 24 hours if we prioritize. We'll include: SOC scope diagram (PDF), latest policy snapshots (Access Control, Data Retention), auditor statement on scope continuity, and control-owner attestation emails.

Ethan Park
Director, Security & Trust — Redwood Inference

Attachment: bridge_letter_draft.docx (stub)

From: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>
To: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-10-11T12:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: SOC 2 coverage dates for procurement — FirstBank (commercial position)

Folks — appreciate the quick triage. From a commercial standpoint, we need to keep the procurement queue moving. Two options to present to FirstBank immediately:

A) Accelerated bridge path (recommended): We provide the bridge/interim attestation + evidence pack (as Ethan outlined) within 48 hours. In parallel we add a 30-day SLA credit clause in the MSA that reduces first-month charges if they experience an availability SLA breach during the first 90 days.

B) Contract hold with concession: If procurement insists on full retro SOC2, offer an extended pricing lock for 90 days and a 5% first-year credit to cover their time while we complete audit follow-up (not my preferred route).

Vanessa — if FirstBank confirms they will accept a bridge letter, we should pursue A immediately. I authorize Legal to sign off on the bridge letter template and the Sales ops team to add the SLA credit clause (low cost, high deal preservation). Marissa — coordinate with Finance to ensure the billing amendment is routable in our contract system.

Vivek Kulkarni
Head of Sales — Redwood Inference

(Short reply OK)

From: Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwoodinference.com>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwoodinference.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwoodinference.com>, Sarah Keller <sarah.keller@firstbankcorp.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-10-12T14:02:00-07:00
Subject: Bridge attestation and evidence pack — FirstBank (final)

Hi all — I pulled together a bridge attestation template and included the minimum evidence index we can reasonably assemble within 48 hours. See summary below and attached draft.

What we're delivering:
- Signed bridge attestation (PDF) on Redwood letterhead (Legal + Auditor acknowledgement block) stating that control design and operating effectiveness are materially consistent across the 2025 and 2026 audit periods for controls in scope.
- Evidence pack (zipped): SOC scope diagram (scope.pdf), Access_Control_Policy_v3.pdf, Incident_Response_Policy_v2.pdf, Control_Owner_Attestations_emails.pdf.
- Contract addendum: 30-day availability credit clause to be appended to the MSA (we propose this as an assurance while the customer processes the attestation).

Notes and next steps:
1) Ethan — please finalize the auditor acknowledgement paragraph and confirm the auditor is willing to be named in the attestation (I penciled in the auditor firm but will remove if they decline).
2) Vanessa/Sarah — if FirstBank confirms they accept the bridge attestation + evidence, I'll send the signed PDF via DocuSign and attach the evidence links.
3) Marissa — please prepare the MSA amendment language; we will include the amendment link in the DocuSign packet.

I'll send the signed bridge letter as soon as Auditor approval comes back. If procurement requires any specific control artifacts beyond the index, flag them now — otherwise we aim to have the complete packet ready by 10/13 EOD.

Best,
Naomi Feldman
Senior Counsel — Redwood Inference

Attachments: bridge_letter_draft.docx (stub), scope.pdf (stub), Access_Control_Policy_v3.pdf (stub), Incident_Response_Policy_v2.pdf (stub)

title: Sequenced provisioning for new teams / Q4 projects
preview:
From: Samir Patel <samir.patel@novacorp.com>
To: Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwood.com>
Cc: Maya Rao <contracts@novacorp.com>
Date: Mon, 05 Oct 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Sequenced provisioning request — Q4 new projects

Hi Maya,

Thanks for the quick intro last week. We have a cluster of new internal projects that need Redwood keys/capacity in a staggered window during Oct–Nov. High level: three product teams (Search, Recommendations, FinEng) will onboard separate apps; we'll need distinct API keys and isolated usage tracking per app.

Key asks:
- Can Redwood provision 3 additional project keys and a staging key per project?
- We expect light staging traffic in week 1 and ramp to ~1.2M tokens/day across all projects by week 6.
- Need an updated cost estimate and any recommended reserved capacity for predictable performance.
- Legal would like a short amendment to our existing contract to add the projects and term extension.

I attached a simple matrix of projects and tentative launch weeks. Can we get a 1–2 line response on feasibility and earliest start date?

Thanks,
Samir
Samir Patel
Head of Platform, NovaCorp
samir.patel@novacorp.com

Attachment: provision_matrix.xlsx (spreadsheet stub)

From: Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwood.com>
To: Samir Patel <samir.patel@novacorp.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 05 Oct 2026 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Sequenced provisioning request — Q4 new projects

Hi Samir — thanks for the matrix, that helps. Good news: we can support separate project keys and per-key billing/metrics. Proposed approach (high level):

Phase 0 (this week): confirm owners & traffic shapes
- We need the PO names and expected p95 latency targets for each app.

Phase 1 (staging keys): provision 3 staging keys (one per project) + a central staging sandbox by Oct 12
- No charge for staging sandbox; staging keys will be rate-limited to protect production capacity.

Phase 2 (prod keys + capacity): staggered prod provisioning over 3 weeks starting Oct 19
- Option A: pay-as-you-go prod keys (no commit)
- Option B: 3-month reserved capacity commit (recommended if you want predictability and 15–20% unit-cost savings).

I've looped in Ben for the cost/commit recommendation. If you want, once we land on reserve vs PAYG we'll draft a small amendment for Legal to review.

Next steps:
- Can you confirm (by EOD Wed): project owners + expected ramp dates and a rough token/day estimate per project?
- Would you prefer we draft the amendment or should your legal start one and we redline?

—Maya
Maya Srinivasan
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
maya.srinivasan@redwood.com
+1 415-555-0126

Attachment referenced: provision_matrix.xlsx (received)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Samir Patel <samir.patel@novacorp.com>, Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 06 Oct 2026 10:15:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Sequenced provisioning request — cost + reserved option

Samir — quick note on cost and a recommended minimal commit to hit p95 SLOs without throttling:

Summary recommendations:
- Short-term (first 4 weeks): PAYG for all three projects to validate traffic patterns.
- Medium-term (if ramp looks like ~1.2M tokens/day by week 6): take a 3-month reserved pool sized at 1.5M tokens/day equivalent. This gives ~18% cost reduction vs PAYG and priority routing during constrained capacity windows.
- We can also set per-key throttles and soft-fallback routing to a lower-cost model if you want further protection against bloom spikes.

Estimated cost (very rough):
- PAYG baseline: $XX,XXX/month (across all projects)
- 3-month reserved (recommended): one-time commit invoiced monthly — see attached estimate for tiered options.

Attachment: dedicated_commit_estimate.pdf (pricing stub)

If you want I can carve a short appendix that lists operational runbooks (key rotation, emergency rollback, contact on-call) and Laura can lead the implementation checklist.

Ben Carter
Strategic Account Manager, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com

Note: pricing figures above are placeholders until we get final traffic profile from NovaCorp.

From: Samir Patel <samir.patel@novacorp.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwood.com>
Cc: Maya Rao <contracts@novacorp.com>
Date: Wed, 07 Oct 2026 08:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Sequenced provisioning request — questions about staging and rollback

Thanks Ben — a few clarifying questions from our eng lead:

1) For staging keys, will those have separate KV caches / zero-impact on prod performance? We want to be sure there is no noisy neighbor effect during early load tests.
2) If we take the reserved pool, can we scope it to only cover the three new projects (i.e., not share with other NovaCorp workloads)?
3) Legal will likely prefer Redwood to draft the amendment so we have a single point of alignment.

App owners:
- Search: Priya Mehta (priya.m@novacorp.com) — target ramp Nov 1
- Recommendations: Owen Lee (owen.l@novacorp.com) — target ramp Nov 8
- FinEng: Clara Ruiz (clara.r@novacorp.com) — target ramp Nov 15

We'll aim to get you a token/day breakdown by EOD Fri.

—Samir

On Tue, Oct 6, 2026 at 10:15 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> Samir — quick note on cost and a recommended minimal commit to hit p95 SLOs without throttling:
> ...


From: Maya Srinivasan <maya.srinivasan@redwood.com>
To: Samir Patel <samir.patel@novacorp.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 08 Oct 2026 15:37:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Sequenced provisioning request — next steps & amendment draft

Samir — appreciate the owner list and target ramps. Summary and commitment from our side:

What we'll do:
- Today: provision three staging keys (Search/Recommendations/FinEng) with isolated meter buckets and separate KV namespaces to avoid noisy neighbor effects. Staging keys active by Oct 12.
- Oct 19, Oct 26, Nov 2: staggered prod key provisioning aligned to your ramp dates (Search -> Recommendations -> FinEng).
- If you confirm the token/day numbers by EOD Fri, Ben will finalize the pricing tier and we will activate a recommended 3-month reserved pool scoped to only the three new projects (per your ask).
- I'll draft a 1-page amendment to your contract (add projects + extend term) and share a DocuSign link by COB Fri for legal review.

Immediate owners and actions:
- Samir: share token/day estimates by EOD Fri (owner-provided).
- Ben: finalize pricing and deliver the committed estimate PDF.
- Laura: build the implementation checklist and staged runbook (key provisioning, rotation schedule, rollback play).
- Ethan: will be on-call the first two weeks of live ramp for monitoring/alerts.

Attachments: amendment_draft.docx (draft will be uploaded to the shared link); provision_matrix.xlsx (referenced).

DocuSign link (draft): https://redwood.share/amendments/NOVA-1198
HubSpot record: https://hubspot.company/contacts/12345

If that all looks good I’ll send the amendment draft shortly and confirm staging keys.

Thanks — looking forward to getting your teams live.

Best,
Maya Srinivasan
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
maya.srinivasan@redwood.com
+1 415-555-0126

--
Laura Bennett will take lead on implementation; Ethan Park will own monitoring on-call during initial ramp.

title: GPU runway audit + soft-hold request for AxiomLabs enterprise close
preview:
From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: Tue, 28 Sep 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: GPU runway audit + soft-hold request for AxiomLabs enterprise close

Team —

AxiomLabs (DEAL-4227) moved to final legal and procurement this morning. They signalled intent to go live in ~6–8 weeks post-SLA, and procurement asked us for a firm commitment window for Dedicated capacity prior to issuing a PO.

Quick ask: can infra ops commit a soft-hold for a 6-week runway on a dedicated pool sized for the expected POC->prod ramp while we finish procurement? High-level requirements from their infra lead: sustained B100/H100 class inference for 3,000 qps of small-chat (avg 64 tokens) and a periodic heavy-batch for nightly embedding jobs (approx 200k tokens/min). The attached snapshot (axiom_capacity_snapshot_q3.xlsx) contains their stated QPS profile and our initial fit.

What I need by EOD:
- Karthik: sizing recommendation (GPU model mix + node count) and suggested thermal/affinity constraints we should reserve.
- Ben: ops checklist for a soft-hold (how many reserve slots, what enforcement mechanism, and what the rollback policy looks like).
- Neha: a short cadence note to procurement + legal we can share with Axiom asking for a tentative PO timeline (so we can decide hold duration).

Goal is to avoid overcommitting nodes we need for other Q4 closes while still giving Axiom confidence to proceed. If we can soft-hold 40–48 H100-equivalent slots for 6 weeks with an automatic release after a signed PO, that would be ideal.

Thanks —
Ethan
Ethan Park
Senior Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference
ethan.park@redwood.com
(attachment: axiom_capacity_snapshot_q3.xlsx)

From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 28 Sep 2026 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: GPU runway audit + soft-hold request for AxiomLabs enterprise close

Ethan —

Thanks for the context and the snapshot. Quick read: the profile maps to two distinct workload shapes — low-latency inference (chat) and high-throughput nightly embeddings. My recommendation:

Sizing proposal (conservative):
- Dedicated pool: 24 nodes with 2x H100 per node (48 H100-equivalent GPUs).
- Software config: continuous small-batch routing for chat with dynamic KV caching; nightly batching window with pre-warm (we should schedule pre-warm 15m before batch start).
- Thermal/affinity notes: place these nodes in a single AZ for predictable latency; mark 20% of capacity as \"ephemeral overflow\" for short bursts if we can allow autoscale-in on top of the hold.

Alternative cost-optimized:
- 32 A100-equivalent slots (mix of quantized weights + aggressive KV caching) — lower latency predictability but cheaper.

For a soft-hold, I concur with your 6-week window. If ops can implement a hold token that prevents allocation to other customers and releases automatically on receipt of the PO/DocuSign callback, that would be ideal.

I'll attach a small MOP sketch in a follow-up.

—Karthik
Karthik Iyer
Principal, Dedicated Capacity Planning
karthik.iyer@redwood.com

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
Cc: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 28 Sep 2026 14:28:00 -0700
Subject: Re: GPU runway audit + soft-hold request for AxiomLabs enterprise close

Karthik / Ethan —

Operationally we can implement a \"soft-hold\" as follows:

1) Create a labeled reservation (RES-Axiom-DEAL4227) tied to customer id that marks N nodes as reserved at the orchestration layer. Reservation semantics: prevents any new tenant allocations but does not evict currently running jobs.
2) Enforcement: scheduler will reject allocation attempts that conflict with reserved capacity unless an override token from infra SRE is provided.
3) Release policy: automatic release 6 weeks from reservation timestamp OR on DocuSign webhook (we have a sample callback URL).
4) Fallback: if production traffic exceeds 95% of reserved capacity for >10 minutes, trigger a policy that either (a) request approval to burst into ephemeral pool (billing as overage) or (b) send immediate escalation to the sales/ops channel.

Estimated impact on other pipelines: reserving 48 H100-eq will reduce our flexible pool by ~12% for the affected region; acceptable but we should log this to the capacity board.

Attachment: proposed-mop.pdf (short runbook).

Ben
Ben Carter
Head of Capacity Ops, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com
(attachment: proposed-mop.pdf)

From: Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
To: Leah Torres <leah.torres@axiomlabs.com>, procurement@axiomlabs.com
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 29 Sep 2026 16:04:00 -0700
Subject: Re: GPU runway audit + soft-hold request for AxiomLabs enterprise close

Leah / Axiom procurement —

Thanks for the update this morning. Redwood is prepared to provision a soft-hold reservation for DEAL-4227 as detailed below so your procurement team can proceed with internal signoff:

- Reservation: RES-Axiom-DEAL4227, 48 H100-equivalent GPUs (24 nodes x 2 GPUs)
- Hold window: 6 weeks from reservation creation date; automatic release on receipt of signed PO or at end of window
- Release trigger: DocuSign webhook or signed PDF to procurement@redwood.com
- Billing: no charge while on soft-hold; billing begins on PO effective date or earlier if the reservation is converted to active usage.

Please let us know an expected PO approval date or if procurement needs us to accept any provisional terms so we can kick off the reservation. Attaching the MOP and a DocuSign link for an optional provisional acknowledgement that would lock the reservation earlier: https://app.docusign.com/AXIOM-4227

Regards —
Neha Kapoor
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference
neha.kapoor@redwood.com

Hi Quantasy AP team,

Attaching invoice INV-2026-1479 for August usage. We want to make this as frictionless as possible for you — two quick options below and a small request on remittance details: 

1) ACH (preferred): please initiate ACH to the account listed in the attached redwood_billing_instructions.pdf. If you use ACH, can you please include the invoice number and \"Redwood Inference, Inc.\" in your payment reference so our cash application team can auto-apply?

2) Virtual card / pay portal: if you prefer a card, we can generate a one-time pay link. Note: virtual card fees are borne by the payer; let us know if you'd like us to proceed.

If neither is feasible, we can accept wire — instructions in the attachment.

Marissa (AE) is copied to confirm PO mapping (if you need a PO on-file) and Ben (collections) is on the thread for any payment coordination.

Best,
Ethan Park

Redwood Inference

• Terms: Net 30 from invoice date (see invoice header). If you need net45 for this payment run, let us know and we can flag it with our finance team, but standard is net30.
• W-9: attached as W9_Redwood_Inference.pdf
• Virtual card: yes, we accept virtual cards. If you route via card, please note any card processing fees will be reflected as a separate line item on your AP reconciliation; alternatively, we can issue a one-time pay link that supports P-Card and sends a payment confirmation back to you.

Request re remittance: when submitting ACH/wire please include the invoice number INV-2026-1479 AND the entity \"Redwood Inference, Inc.\" in the reference/notes field. That ensures auto-application and avoids manual matching delays. If your AP system requires different remittance tagging, please tell us and Ben will provide a tailored remittance pad.
