From: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 8 Sep 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Confidential: coaching engagement plan for Ethan Park

Hi Monica / Laura,

I want to flag an ongoing performance concern with Ethan Park on the platform reliability squad and propose a short, documented coaching engagement before we consider formal HR steps. I know we need to be deliberate and consistent here — I’m aiming for a focused, evidence-backed approach so the employee has a fair chance to correct course.

Snapshot of issues (examples from last 60 days):
- Missed two feature delivery dates (service-mesh rollout) by 12 and 9 business days.
- Multiple PRs with unresolved review comments (average turnaround >72 hrs) causing rework downstream.
- Incident postmortem had gaps (no clear action owner for regression).
- Code quality regressions in integration tests (three returned defects in staging).

Proposal (draft; attached): coaching_plan_draft.pdf
- Duration: 30 days (starting 2026-09-13) with weekly 1:1 checkpoints and specific measurable goals.
- Goals:
  * Delivery: meet next sprint deliverable (merge + deploy) by 2026-10-08 without backlog carryover for assigned tasks.
  * Reviews: PR response time <48 hours; reduce comment rework by 50% (tracked via PR threads).
  * Reliability & ownership: own two incident follow-ups to completion (documented actions).
- Support: buddying with Laura for technical scope decisions, weekly syncs with me for blockers, and HR will monitor cadence/notes.
- Consequence: if goals are not met by end of period we will escalate to formal corrective action (PIP) — intent is not punitive but to be clear about next steps.

Questions for HR: is the wording and timeline here aligned with People Ops guidance? Anything I should add to the documentation (calibration steps, required signatures, or legal language)?

I’m happy to revise the draft with HR input and then deliver to Ethan in a private 1:1. I’d like to keep this thread strictly confidential.

Thanks,
Diego
Diego Fernandez
Engineering Manager, Platform Reliability
Redwood Inference

Attachment: coaching_plan_draft.pdf (pdf)


From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
To: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 8 Sep 2026 10:04:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Confidential: coaching engagement plan for Ethan Park

Diego — thanks for the clear summary and for documenting examples. I reviewed the draft quickly and attached a short People Ops guidance doc (hr_guidance_pre-pip.docx) with suggested edits. High-level feedback: 

1) Tone and scope: keep the plan framed as coaching with measurable checkpoints. Avoid wording that could be construed as immediate disciplinary action — we should reserve \"PIP\" language for the calibrated step after documented failure to meet objectives.

2) Evidence & examples: add links to the specific PRs / incident postmortem pages in the docs so we can trace metrics.

3) Sign-off & calibration: schedule a calibration with Laura (as Diego’s skip-level) and a member of People Ops before the plan is presented to Ethan. A quick 30-min sync is fine.

4) Meetings & notes: HR should be copied on the 1:1 notes (summary email) after each weekly check-in. We’ll keep the notes in the private People folder.

5) Reasonable accommodations: before moving forward, confirm whether anything outside work (leave, medical, or other) might be affecting performance — this is a standard check we do.

6) Timing: 30 days is acceptable for targeted behavioral corrections; we typically document a 30/60 cadence for technical contributors where necessary.

Suggested edits included in the attached doc. I can join the first 1:1 when you present the plan. Keep this limited to Diego / Laura / People Ops / Ethan.

Monica
Monica Patel
People Ops Partner
Redwood Inference

Attachment: hr_guidance_pre-pip.docx (docx)


From: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 13 Sep 2026 11:00:00 -07:00
Subject: 1:1 — Coaching & expectations (private)

Ethan — I wanted to follow up from our recent chats and share a proposed short-term coaching plan so we have a shared set of expectations. Monica is copied to help keep things documented and consistent.

Summary (what I’ve observed):
- Delivery dates missed on two key tickets (service-mesh feature).
- PR turnaround and response cadence slower than team norms.
- Incident follow-ups need clearer owners and completion artifacts.

What I want to see over the next 30 days (start: 2026-09-13):
- Deliverable ownership: complete the outstanding service-mesh tasks and get the remaining work merged by 2026-10-08.
- PRs: respond to review requests within 48 hours and close review threads within 5 business days unless blocked.
- Reliability: document and close two incident follow-up action items (assign owners + date).

Support I’ll provide:
- Weekly 30-min check-ins (Tuesdays, 10:30am) to unblock and track progress.
- Laura will act as technical buddy for scope decisions where needed.
- I will escalate resourcing or blocking issues immediately if they impact deadlines.

I attached a one-page checklist for our 1:1s (1on1_expectations.xlsx). Please review and let me know if you have questions. Let’s meet on 2026-09-14 at 10:30am — if that doesn’t work tell me an alternative this week.

This is meant to be constructive and time-boxed; we’ll check progress weekly and document outcomes.

Thanks,
Diego

Attachment: 1on1_expectations.xlsx (xlsx)


From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
To: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 14 Sep 2026 09:05:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: 1:1 — Coaching & expectations (private)

Diego — thanks for the note and for laying out the expectations. I want to be upfront: I know I missed timelines and I take responsibility. Two quick asks before the meeting:

1) Can you drop the PR links and the incident references into the doc so I have the exact context? It will help me prepare.
2) I’ve had some personal scheduling constraints this month — can we move the initial check-in to 2026-09-16 at 11:00am? I want to go into the meeting prepared and with proposed mitigation steps.

I appreciate the support.

— Ethan


From: Diego Fernandez <diego.fernandez@redwood.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 24 Sep 2026 15:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: 1:1 — Coaching & expectations (private)

Ethan — 2026-09-16 at 11:00am works; calendar invite sent. I’ve attached the final coaching plan (final_coaching_plan_20260924.pdf) which includes the PR links and incident notes you requested, plus the weekly check-in template we’ll use.

Quick next steps:
- 2026-09-16: Kickoff 1:1 (Monica will join to take notes and confirm documentation).
- Weekly check-ins every Tuesday at 10:30am (we’ll cancel if blocked) — first progress summary due 2026-09-23.
- End of period review: 2026-10-13 — we’ll assess whether objectives were met and agree next steps.

Thanks for being open — we’ll walk through concrete actions in the meeting and I’ll share the meeting notes after each check-in.

Diego

Attachment: final_coaching_plan_20260924.pdf (pdf)

title: Marketplace deal economics — rebate carveout & channel passback evidence
preview:
From: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.com>
To: Marta Ruiz <marta.ruiz@acadiahealth.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 08 Sep 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Marketplace deal economics — rebate carveout & channel passback evidence

Hi Marta,

Thanks again for the time today. Per our discussion I wanted to capture the open questions we need to close before we finalize the private-offer economics for Acadia Health (ACD-2026-4721):

1) Rebate slab definition — you mentioned a 12–24 month commit with tiered volume rebates. Can you confirm which usage buckets you expect to count toward the tier (monthly consumption vs committed reserved hours)?

2) Channel passback — your procurement team needs a clear invoice-level passback path to the reseller (they called this "distributor visibility"). We typically provide a monthly reconciliation extract plus a mapping doc showing how the marketplace settlement flows map back to reseller POs. I can attach an example workbook — will that satisfy finance?

3) Evidence for audit — please list exactly which artifacts procurement will require for the passback (invoice PDFs, marketplace settlement CSV, signed reseller contract). We want to avoid surprises in the approval cycle.

4) Implementation timing — if we include a carveout for certain SKUs (e.g., reserved capacity), do you need a contract amendment or is a marketplace private-offer note sufficient?

I'll prepare a short example calculation (illustrative numbers) and a one-page flow diagram that maps settlement → reseller → Acadia. Attaching those and looping in Jordan (channel ops) and Naomi (finance) so we can align on the specific artifacts.

Next step: if you can reply with the list of artifacts and the preferred bucket definition by EOD Friday (9/11), we will produce a reconciled workbook for your procurement and run it by the AWS marketplace rep for confirmation.

Thanks,

Ethan Park
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ethan_park@redwood.com | +1 415-555-0186

Attachment: (planned) acadia_rebate_model_v2.xlsx (xlsx) — sample calculation; marketplace_passback_flow.pdf (pdf) — settlement map


From: Marta Ruiz <marta.ruiz@acadiahealth.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 09 Sep 2026 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Marketplace deal economics — rebate carveout & channel passback evidence

Hi Ethan,

Thanks — quick clarifications from procurement here:

- Buckets: Acadia counts committed reserved hours toward tiering; any ad-hoc overage (on-demand) should be excluded unless explicitly committed. For example, a 1-year reserved commitment should count 100% of the committed node-hours.

- Artifacts: procurement will need (a) monthly invoice PDFs showing reseller line items, (b) AWS marketplace settlement CSV for the same months, and (c) a signed reseller agreement amendment that references passback/commission language.

- Distributor: We use a regional distributor (HealthResell, NA). They insist on a line-level passback on the invoice; a separate shadow report is acceptable but must reconcile to the invoice.

Finally, can you show an example where the rebate is applied net-of-reseller-commission (so Acadia understands net economic benefit)? If you could share that by Friday, procurement can take it to legal.

Best,

Marta Ruiz
Director, Procurement — Acadia Health
marta.ruiz@acadiahealth.com | +1 212-555-0102


From: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.com>
To: Marta Ruiz <marta.ruiz@acadiahealth.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 10 Sep 2026 08:25:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Marketplace deal economics — rebate carveout & channel passback evidence

Marta — thanks, noted.

Attaching the requested artifacts: acadia_rebate_model_v2.xlsx (worked example with net-of-reseller-commission rows) and marketplace_passback_flow.pdf (one-page flow diagram mapping the settlement csv to invoice lines). High-level notes below and a short worked example inside the workbook.

Summary of the sample calculation (row references from the workbook):
- Committed hours: 10,000 node-hours/month at list price $0.20/hr → gross list $2,000/month.
- Reseller commission: 12% of list → $240 retained by reseller.
- Rebate slab applied at 10% net-of-commission → rebate $176 (10% of net $1,760).
- Net to customer after rebate: $2,000 - $240 - $176 = $1,584/month.

We include a reconciliation tab showing how the AWS settlement CSV maps to the invoice line — marketplace SKU → private-offer SKU → invoice line id. That mapping is what HealthResell needs to show the passback on their reseller invoice.

Next steps: 1) If procurement agrees to the net-of-reseller treatment, Naomi and I will draft the amendment language. 2) We'll engage the AWS marketplace rep for a short confirmation on settlement timing and the specific settlement field names used for mapping. I'm + Jordan on scheduling a 30-min call with HealthResell next week.

Cheers,

Ethan Park
Account Executive | Redwood Inference

Attachments: acadia_rebate_model_v2.xlsx, marketplace_passback_flow.pdf

On Wed, Sep 9, 2026 at 11:03 AM Marta Ruiz <marta.ruiz@acadiahealth.com> wrote:

> 
> Thanks — quick clarifications from procurement here:
> 
> - Buckets: Acadia counts committed reserved hours toward tiering; any ad-hoc overage (on-demand) should be excluded unless explicitly committed. For example, a 1-year reserved commitment should count 100% of the committed node-hours.
> 
> - Artifacts: procurement will need (a) monthly invoice PDFs showing reseller line items, (b) AWS marketplace settlement CSV for the same months, and (c) a signed reseller agreement amendment that references passback/commission language.
> 
> - Distributor: We use a regional distributor (HealthResell, NA). They insist on a line-level passback on the invoice; a separate shadow report is acceptable but must reconcile to the invoice.
> 
> Finally, can you show an example where the rebate is applied net-of-reseller-commission (so Acadia understands net economic benefit)? If you could share that by Friday, procurement can take it to legal.
> 
> Best,
> 
> Marta


From: Jacob Lin <aws-marketplace@amazon.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.com>, Marta Ruiz <marta.ruiz@acadiahealth.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 14 Sep 2026 10:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Marketplace deal economics — rebate carveout & channel passback evidence

Hi all — looping in some AWS marketplace detail to make sure the mapping is clean for settlement. A couple of operational points: 

1) Settlement cadence: AWS typically posts settlements on a monthly cadence with a settlement CSV that contains SKU-level charges, charges-basis, and the partner/fiscal identifier. The CSV includes both list and the marketplace discount fields; you should be able to match the partner_fiscal_id to HealthResell for reconciliation.

2) Invoice mapping: Private offer line items can include a field that points back to the private-offer ID. If you populate that on the invoice line, the settlement CSV will contain the private-offer reference for easier mapping.

3) Evidence: For audit-friendly mapping send (a) the settlement CSV, (b) the reseller invoice PDF, and (c) the private-offer definition (json/pdf) that shows which SKUs and pricing applied. If you want, I can run a short extract of last month's settlement for the private-offer test account — send me the private-offer ID.

Happy to join a call if useful.

Regards,
Jacob Lin
AWS Marketplace — Distribution Partnerships
aws-marketplace@amazon.com


From: Marta Ruiz <marta.ruiz@acadiahealth.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.com>, Jacob Lin <aws-marketplace@amazon.com>
Cc: Jordan Blake <jordan_blake@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi_feldman@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 21 Sep 2026 15:30:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Marketplace deal economics — rebate carveout & channel passback evidence

Ethan / Jacob — thanks for the clear examples and the AWS confirmation. Procurement has reviewed the workbook and confirms the net-of-reseller treatment is acceptable so long as HealthResell is named in the amendment and the monthly invoice includes a line that can be reconciled to the settlement CSV.

Next steps from Acadia:
- Please prepare a short amendment (1 page) that: names HealthResell as the reseller, states rebate slabs net-of-reseller-commission, and specifies the settlement artifacts required monthly.
- Please share the private-offer ID (so Jacob can extract the last full settlement) and we'll route a DocuSign to our legal team.

If you can send the amendment draft and the private-offer ID by EOD Thursday (9/23), we will target signature the following week.

Marta

- Committed hours: 10,000 node-hours/month at list price $0.20/hr → gross list $2,000/month.
- Reseller commission: 12% of list → $240 retained by reseller.
- Rebate slab applied at 10% net-of-commission → rebate $176 (10% of net $1,760).
- Net to customer after rebate: $2,000 - $240 - $176 = $1,584/month.

We include a reconciliation tab showing how the AWS settlement CSV maps to the invoice line — marketplace SKU → private-offer SKU → invoice line id. That mapping is what HealthResell needs to show the passback on their reseller invoice.

Next steps: 1) If procurement agrees to the net-of-reseller treatment, Naomi and I will draft the amendment language. 2) We'll engage the AWS marketplace rep for a short confirmation on settlement timing and the specific settlement field names used for mapping. I'm + Jordan on scheduling a 30-min call with HealthResell next week.

Cheers,

Ethan Park

title: Unified rerank POC — technical checklist & next steps
preview:
From: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
To: Phil Barnes <phil.barnes@searchlytics.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 7 Sep 2026 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Unified rerank POC — technical checklist & next steps

Hi Phil / Maya —

Thanks again for the call this morning. Quick recap from my side plus the technical checklist Laura / Ethan will need to scope the POC: 

Summary of needs (from SearchLytics):
- Reranking layer after first-pass retrieval for product search (latency SLO: 120ms p95 at 50 qps)
- Support for dense embeddings as features + sparse term signals
- Ability to evaluate multiple ranking signals and tune top_k and reranker depth
- Auditability: per-request scoring and trace for model selection and routing

Technical checklist for the POC (items we can deliver / need from you):
1) SDK & auth: Redwood SDK installed, API key with sandbox scope OR service-account JWT for server-to-server auth (please confirm preferred approach).
2) Input format: we need a CSV sample of query + candidate doc ids + retrieval scores (see attached searchlytics-poc-queries.csv).
3) Output contract: JSON signed schema with fields: doc_id, score, reason_tokens[] or structured_explanation (we can support function-calling style structured output).
4) Routing & variants: confirm if you want a single rerank model or an A/B between a smaller quantized variant and a full-precision variant.
5) Top_k tuning: expected candidate pool size (k) and target re-rank depth (we recommend re-rank top 50 then return top 10).
6) Evaluation metrics: please confirm primary metric (MRR@10 / nDCG@10 / NDCG@5) and any manual relevance labels you have.
7) Privacy/compliance: whether any PII is present in candidate docs and whether we should enable private deployment (VPC).

Next steps (proposed):
- Phil: share the sample CSV and which auth approach you prefer (API key vs JWT) — by end of day Wed.
- Laura / Ethan: we'll draft a short POC plan and target dates once we get the CSV and confirm auth.

Attachment: redwood-rerank-proposal.pdf (POC scope & pricing stub)
Attachment: searchlytics-poc-queries.csv (sample rows requested)

Thanks — happy to hop on a short follow-up if you prefer to walk through the checklist.

Best,
Priyom Das
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
priyom.das@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0192



From: Phil Barnes <phil.barnes@searchlytics.com>
To: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
Cc: Maya Rao <maya@searchlytics.com>
Date: Tue, 7 Sep 2026 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Unified rerank POC — technical checklist & next steps

Thanks Priyom — good summary. Quick answers inline and a couple of follow-ups: 

Auth: we prefer server-to-server JWTs (we already issue service tokens via our infra team).
CSV: attached a preliminary export (few hundred rows) — will send a larger anonymized set by Friday.
Top_k: our current retrieval returns top 200; product team thinks re-ranking top 100 and returning top 20 makes sense. Can you confirm cost/latency tradeoffs for re-rank depth 100 vs 50?
Metrics: primaries are MRR@10 and nDCG@10; secondary is CTR lift on search sessions. We have a small human-labeled set (~500 queries) for offline eval.
PII: candidate docs include product descriptions and some vendor contact names (no consumer PII). VPC not required for POC.

Also Ethan — can you confirm whether the structured_explanation format can include token-level highlights or just coarse reasons? We want to display highlighted spans in the UI.

-Phil

Attachment: searchlytics-poc-queries.csv (preliminary sample, small)


From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
To: Phil Barnes <phil.barnes@searchlytics.com>, Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 7 Sep 2026 13:21:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Unified rerank POC — technical checklist & next steps

Hey Phil — quick technical notes on structured_explanation and performance tradeoffs: 

1) Structured explanation format — what we can support in the POC:
- token_highlights: array of {doc_id, token_offsets:[start,end], score_contrib} (this is more expensive but doable as an opt-in feature)
- coarse_reasons: array of strings with attribution buckets ("semantic_match", "title_boost", "recency") — cheaper and small payloads
We can include token_highlights for the top 10 returned docs by default; that keeps the extra latency bounded.

2) Latency vs depth: re-ranking top 100 will cost ~2.2x tokens processed vs top 50 in our benchmarks on comparable models; with batching and prefix caching we still expect p95 around 110–140ms for optimized quantized variants at 50 qps. We'll run a short microbench once we pick model variants.

3) Ranking API shape: we will expose a /v1/rerank endpoint that accepts: query, candidates (list of {doc_id, features, retrieval_score}), context flags, and returns an ordered list with scores and optional structured_explanation. We also support streaming partial results for UI-first experiences.

4) Evaluation: we recommend MRR@10 + nDCG@10 as you specified and adding pairwise loss curves during training to monitor stability. We'll ingest your human labels and compute offline metrics. For A/B we'll instrument CTR and search conversion as the online metric.

I can mock a small /v1/rerank call with your CSV if you want — drop the bigger anonymized set and I'll run a baseline.

—Ethan
Senior ML Engineer, Redwood Inference
ethan.park@redwood.ai


From: Priyom Das <priyom.das@redwood.ai>
To: Phil Barnes <phil.barnes@searchlytics.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 8 Sep 2026 09:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Unified rerank POC — technical checklist & next steps

Phil — thanks, that all helps. A couple of organizational items so we move quickly: 

1) Auth flow: we can accept JWTs. We'll need a public JWK endpoint from your infra so we can validate signatures; alternatively we can provision a short-lived Redwood API key scoped to the POC if you prefer zero infra changes.
2) POC timeline: propose the following sprint: 
   - Week 0: get full anonymized CSV + JWK or confirm API key path
   - Week 1: baseline reranker run + offline metrics (Ethan)
   - Week 2: integrate structured_explanation (token_highlights opt-in) + A/B harness
   - Week 3: live canary on 1% of traffic (switch to UI)
3) Deliverables: baseline metrics artifact, model variant configs (quantized vs fp), cost/latency estimate, sample API contract (we'll send a swagger snippet).

Laura — can you draft a short POC SOW and a scoped list of API requests we will support (inputs/outputs + error codes)? Aim for EOD Thursday so we can get sign-off from SearchLytics.

Phil — confirm whether the JWK endpoint is available or if you'd like the API key path. Also confirm the final sized dataset (~5–10k queries preferred).

Thanks — Priyom

Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 9 Sep 2026 16:02:00 -0700

Phil

5) Top_k tuning: expected candidate pool size (k) and target re-rank depth (we recommend re-rank top 50 then return top 10).
6) Evaluation metrics: please confirm primary metric (MRR@10 / nDCG@10 / NDCG@5) and any manual relevance labels you have.
7) Privacy/compliance: whether any PII is present in candidate docs and whether we should enable private deployment (VPC).

From: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.ai>

Subject: Re: Unified rerank POC — technical checklist & next steps

Hey Phil — quick technical notes on structured_explanation and performance tradeoffs: 

1) Structured explanation format — what we can support in the POC:
- token_highlights: array of {doc_id, token_offsets:[start,end], score_contrib} (this is more expensive but doable as an opt-in feature)
- coarse_reasons: array of strings with attribution buckets ("semantic_match", "title_boost", "recency") — cheaper and small payloads
We can include token_highlights for the top 10 returned docs by default; that keeps the extra latency bounded.

2) Latency vs depth: re-ranking top 100 will cost ~2.2x tokens processed vs top 50 in our benchmarks on comparable models; with batching and prefix caching we still expect p95 around 110–140ms for optimized quantized variants at 50 qps. We'll run a short microbench once we pick model variants.

3) Ranking API shape: we will expose a /v1/rerank endpoint that accepts: query, candidates (list of {doc_id, features, retrieval_score}), context flags, and returns an ordered list with scores and optional structured_explanation. We also support streaming partial results for UI-first experiences.

4) Evaluation: we recommend MRR@10 + nDCG@10 as you specified and adding pairwise loss curves during training to monitor stability. We'll ingest your human labels and compute offline metrics. For A/B we'll instrument CTR and search conversion as the online metric.

I can mock a small /v1/rerank call with your CSV if you want — drop the bigger anonymized set and I'll run a baseline.

—Ethan

ethan.park@redwood.ai

title: Private endpoint outbound policy — safelist evidence & firewall scoping
preview:
From: Timothy Hooper <tim.hooper@northernclinics.com>
To: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>
Cc: Nitya Patel <nitya.patel@northernclinics.com>
Date: Fri, 4 Sep 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Private endpoint egress proof / firewall scoping request

Hi Mina,

Thanks for taking this. As part of our security review for the private deployment (VPC) we need a compact set of artifacts to close outbound/network questions with our audit team. Can you confirm the following for the Redwood Private endpoint we'll be using?

- Canonical list of outbound IPs (or IP ranges) required for the control plane and model endpoints.
- Whether DNS or SNI allowlisting is required in addition to IPs.
- Any egress proxy / mTLS expectations for the control plane.
- Recommended firewall rules (ports, proto) and any required hostnames for cert validation.

If possible please share a short spreadsheet or CSV we can attach to our compliance ticket. Our audit window closes next Thursday (2026-09-10) so earlier is better.

Also looped Nitya (security lead) who will validate.

Appreciate it —
Tim
Procurement & Security Ops
Northern Clinics


From: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>
To: Timothy Hooper <tim.hooper@northernclinics.com>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 4 Sep 2026 11:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Private endpoint egress proof / firewall scoping request

Tim —

Thanks for the clear checklist. Attaching an initial CSV with the allowlist for the control plane and a short egress matrix. High level answers below; detailed notes in the attachments.

Quick answers:
- IPs: control plane (TLS management/control): 34.216.192.0/27, 52.8.64.128/26. Model endpoints (regional, set A): 18.205.128.0/24 and 3.134.64.0/24. We provide both stable pools and a secondary ephemeral pool for burst traffic — both are in the CSV.
- DNS/SNI: Not strictly required for basic allowlisting if you permit the IP ranges, but we strongly recommend permitting SNI-based hostnames (*.redwood-inference.com) in environments that do HTTPS SNI inspection so TLS validation to our certs succeeds.
- mTLS/egress proxy: mTLS to our control plane is optional; we support it for customers who require mutual auth. For most private deployments customers use an outbound proxy with TLS pass-through. If you need mTLS we can share client certs and an onboarding key exchange process.
- Ports: HTTPS (443) only for both control and model endpoints. Some customers also open 80 for health-check redirects but not required.

Attachments included: redwood_allowed_ips.csv (IP lists + region tag), controlplane-egress-matrix.xlsx (rules, SNI/hostnames, notes). I also created a short evidence bundle we're assembling and will drop a zip by Monday.

Next steps we propose:
1) You confirm whether you will rely on IP allowlist only or also on SNI hostnames.
2) If you require mTLS, we schedule a short onboarding call and exchange certs.
3) We'll deliver a zipped evidence bundle (signed CSV + timestamped cert thumbprints) by 2026-09-07.

If helpful, Ethan (infra) and Laura (security) are on-thread to add technical specifics.

—Mina
Sr. Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference


From: Timothy Hooper <tim.hooper@northernclinics.com>
To: Mina Haddad <mina.haddad@redwood.com>
Cc: Nitya Patel <nitya.patel@northernclinics.com>
Date: Mon, 7 Sep 2026 08:24:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Private endpoint egress proof / firewall scoping request

Thanks Mina — that's useful. A couple of follow-ups from our security team after reviewing the CSV:

- Can you confirm TTL/rotation cadence for the ephemeral pool? We cannot have frequent unexpected IP changes without a notification SLA.
- Our firewall team performs SNI + certificate fingerprinting. Do you have stable cert thumbprints for the control plane and a published rotation schedule we can pin to?
- For internal auditing we need a signed artifact saying these IPs were valid as of the timestamp — will the evidence bundle include a signature and a date?

If the answers are straightforward we can close the ticket. If not, Nitya suggested a short 30-min call this week to get final signoff.

To: Timothy Hooper <tim.hooper@northernclinics.com>
Cc: Ethan Park <ethan.park@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 7 Sep 2026 15:42:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Private endpoint egress proof / firewall scoping request

Tim, Nitya —

Short answers to your three items and next steps:

- Ephemeral pool rotation: Ephemeral ranges rotate on a 180-day cadence by default; we provide a notification and a signed rotation manifest 14 days prior to change. For customers who need longer windows we can pin an allocation for the term of the contract (request via account rep).
- Cert thumbprints & rotation: We publish control-plane certificate thumbprints for the active cert bundle. Thumbprints are included in the evidence bundle; primary cert rotations are scheduled quarterly and we publish a 30-day notice for planned rotations.
- Signed artifact: Yes — the evidence bundle (safelist-evidence-bundle.zip) will include a signed CSV and a machine-readable JSON manifest with timestamps and cert thumbprints. We'll place this in the Google Drive link and provide a signed PDF for your audit ticket.

Action items:
- Mina / Ethan: upload signed bundle to the Drive link and send final note (by EOD Tue 2026-09-08).
- Nitya / Tim: confirm whether SNI hostnames will be enforced by your perimeter so we can tailor the guidance.

Happy to hop on a 30-min call if you'd rather — Ethan, Laura, please propose 2 slots tomorrow morning PDT.

Thanks —
Mina
Sr. Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
Attachments: redwood_allowed_ips.csv, controlplane-egress-matrix.xlsx, (safelist-evidence-bundle.zip pending upload)

title: HealCo DPA: model-improvement opt-out, prompt & response custody
preview:
From: Marta Lewis <marta.lewis@healco.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.com>
Cc: Procurement Team <procurement@healco.com>
Date: 2026-08-29T09:12:00Z
Subject: DPA questions: model improvement & prompt/response ownership

Hi Ethan —

Thanks for the quick demo last week. Our legal/procurement teams are reviewing the DPA and have a few gating asks before we can move to signature:

• Can Redwood commit to a model-improvement opt-out (no adaptive training) for our tenant data? We need language that is operationally enforceable, not just aspirational.
• We also need clarity on ownership of prompts and generated responses — HealCo must retain exclusive rights to prompts and responses created from our users.
• Please provide a current subprocessor inventory and a short description of where interaction logs may be routed (S3, GCP buckets, onshore/offshore).
• Any evidence you can share about EU residency or export controls for HealCo data would be helpful.

Do you have a short operational summary and suggested contract language that we can feed to legal? Prefer to avoid a long back-and-forth if possible.

Thanks,
Marta
Marta Lewis
Head of Procurement, HealCo


From: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.com>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>
Cc: David Kim <david_kim@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.com>, Marta Lewis <marta.lewis@healco.com>
Date: 2026-08-29T09:45:00Z
Subject: Re: DPA questions: model improvement & prompt/response ownership

Hi Marta —

Appreciate the clear list. Looping in Kimberly (legal) and David (privacy/ops) and Jonas (inference) so we can give a concise operational response and suggested DPA language. Quick answers up front:

• We can offer a tenant-level opt-out from model improvement (no adaptive training) with controls in the deployment configuration and contractual language.
• We can commit to HealCo ownership of prompts + responses; we typically include a clause granting customers exclusive entitlements for customer-provided inputs and generated outputs.
• Subprocessor inventory and data routing details are available; I attached a short redline guide and will follow up with an inventory PDF from Kim.

Action items:
1) Kimberly: can you draft the suggested clause text (short, contract-ready)?
2) David: please confirm the subprocessor list and provide the residency/export statement we can include.
3) Jonas: any operational caveats we should call out (latency vs. cache/batching trade-offs, ephemeral KV handling)?

Marta — we can produce a 1-page operational summary for legal + a short evidence pack (subprocessor list, residency statement, and an outline of how we isolate tenant data). Will that work for your team?

Thanks,
Ethan Park
Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
Attachment: redwood-dpa-redline-guide.pdf (summary of typical DPA clauses and example wording)


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.com>, David Kim <david_kim@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.com>, Marta Lewis <marta.lewis@healco.com>
Date: 2026-08-29T13:02:00Z
Subject: Re: DPA questions: model improvement & prompt/response ownership

Ethan / team / Marta —

Thanks — draft language below and I attached the current subprocessor inventory. This is intentionally short so your counsel can drop it into the DPA.

Draft clause (suggested):

"Customer Data Usage and Model Improvement." Redwood Inference will not use Customer Data to improve, fine-tune, or adapt any machine learning or other models for the benefit of Redwood or third parties without the Customer's prior written consent. For the avoidance of doubt, any model training or improvement performed by Redwood using Customer Data requires an explicit opt-in amendment to this Agreement.

"Ownership of Prompts and Outputs." Customer retains all right, title, and interest in and to any prompts (inputs) and model outputs (responses) provided to or generated by Redwood under this Agreement. Redwood hereby assigns to Customer any rights it may have in such prompts and outputs, and will not use, sell, or otherwise exploit those prompts or outputs outside of the performance of services under this Agreement.

Operational notes for legal:
• We implement the opt-out at the tenant configuration level — toggleable in the control plane; default for enterprise DPA is opt-out (no-training).
• Ephemeral KV/cache: transient artifacts used to serve a session are deleted per retention schedule; we can provide a short retention table.
• Subprocessors: inventory attached; any changes trigger the contractually-agreed notice period.

Attachments: subprocessor-inventory-202608.pdf; redwood-no-train-clause-draft.docx

Kimberly Park
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference


From: Marta Lewis <marta.lewis@healco.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>, David Kim <david_kim@redwood.com>, Procurement Team <procurement@healco.com>
Date: 2026-08-30T15:04:00Z
Subject: Re: DPA questions: model improvement & prompt/response ownership

Ethan / Kimberly —

Thanks — that draft language is exactly what we needed. A few follow-ups from legal/procurement:

• Can you confirm the retention table for ephemeral KV/cache (max retention, deletion cadence)?
• We need a short statement confirming that any cross-border transfers would be governed by SCCs (or equivalent) and which subprocessors process data outside the EU. Please call out the onshore options (US-only routing / EU-only routing) and any lead time required for residency guarantees.
• We will circulate the clause internally and expect to redline minor wording; would prefer to get to DocuSign by next week.

If you can provide the retention table and a one-paragraph residency/export statement, procurement will loop legal and get back with redlines.

Appreciate the quick turnaround.

—Marta

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>
To: Ethan Park <ethan_park@redwood.com>, David Kim <david_kim@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas_weber@redwood.com>, Marta Lewis <marta.lewis@healco.com>
Date: 2026-08-29T13:02:00Z
Subject: Re: DPA questions: model improvement & prompt/response ownership

Ethan / team / Marta —

Thanks — draft language below and I attached the current subprocessor inventory. This is intentionally short so your counsel can drop it into the DPA.

Draft clause (suggested):

"Customer Data Usage and Model Improvement." Redwood Inference will not use Customer Data to improve, fine-tune, or adapt any machine learning or other models for the benefit of Redwood or third parties without the Customer's prior written consent. For the avoidance of doubt, any model training or improvement performed by Redwood using Customer Data requires an explicit opt-in amendment to this Agreement.

"Ownership of Prompts and Outputs." Customer retains all right, title, and interest in and to any prompts (inputs) and model outputs (responses) provided to or generated by Redwood under this Agreement. Redwood hereby assigns to Customer any rights it may have in such prompts and outputs, and will not use, sell, or otherwise exploit those prompts or outputs outside of the performance of services under this Agreement.

Operational notes for legal:
• We implement the opt-out at the tenant configuration level — toggleable in the control plane; default for enterprise DPA is opt-out (no-training).
• Ephemeral KV/cache: transient artifacts used to serve a session are deleted per retention schedule; we can provide a short retention table.
• Subprocessors: inventory attached; any changes trigger the contractually-agreed notice period.

Attachments: subprocessor-inventory-202608.pdf; redwood-no-train-clause-draft.docx

Kimberly Park
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference
